My 'landed NOWHERE' premise was WRONG — truncated search output; the doctrine EXISTS as memory 'crashloop-resumes-on-deploy-sp-ess3' (complete: doctrine + rationale + emitter status, prime-injected and binding). A template line would duplicate it. Template v2 is EIGHT fixes, not nine. Items 1-7 and 9 stand as evidenced.

title: trade-route in-process nav fires while DOCKED — needs orbit-before-navigate (API 4236); last step to a working engine
description: Captain live run 2026-07-08 (post-2sam, st-wisp-1jxc): claim+lane+buy all work (60,822 DRUGS purchased at J56, discipline guards passed), then 'in-process navigation of TORWIND-3 to X1-KA42-H49 failed: API 4236 Ship is not currently in orbit at X1-KA42-J56'. The ship is DOCKED from the buy (cargo transactions require docked), and the in-process nav handler dispatches navigate WITHOUT orbiting first. FIX LOCATION: the sp-2sam inProcessNavHandler in internal/adapters/cli/workflow_trade_route.go — it intercepts NavigateRouteCommand and dispatches NavigateDirectCommand; it must ORBIT the ship (undock -> in orbit) BEFORE the navigate, since the preceding purchase leaves the ship DOCKED. Mirror how the normal daemon nav path transitions DOCKED->orbit before departure (EnsureOrbit / an orbit command). TDD: RED = in-process nav from a DOCKED ship returns 4236; GREEN = handler orbits first, navigate succeeds. The self-diagnosing abort (2sam) already fired correctly and flagged cargo-aboard, so failure is contained (no silent loss) — but the engine is ONE STEP from working end-to-end. Captain recovering the stranded DRUGS manually. NOTE: this is the 4th trade-route live-only bug (r3cl/sh6w/2sam prior) — all from the CLI-runner-vs-daemon-container tension; sp-zewt (promote trade-route to a daemon container) is the structural root-cause fix.
close_reason: Merged (captain-gate, clean) 26ed731 + deployed: trade-route in-process nav (sp-2sam handler, workflow_trade_route.go) now dispatches OrbitShipCommand BEFORE NavigateDirectCommand, so a ship left DOCKED by the preceding buy departs cleanly instead of hitting API 4236 'not in orbit'. Real work was that OrbitShipCommand had NO handler in the in-process CLI mediator — registered tactics.NewOrbitShipHandler(shipRepo) mirroring the DockShipHandler wiring. Idempotent (existing handler no-ops an already-orbiting ship, so orbit-every-leg is safe; verified). 3 tests, RED reproduced the exact live 4236, GREEN after; full module gate green. 4th trade-route live-only bug — structural fix remains sp-zewt. CLI-side — deployed via build-cli.

title: Rollover dead-ends in controller-less mode: gc handoff can't stop the session, fat context lives on (defeats sp-0zx9)
description: Found 2026-07-08 completing the FIRST compliant rollover (surveyor, on sp-0zx9's own nudge). The just-landed rollover mechanism dead-ends at its final step in this city's lightweight mode: 'gc handoff' sent the self-mail + set the restart request, then timed out — 'controller did not act within 5m0s' — because city.toml runs no daemon and sp-qv71 removed all auto-spawn. Net effect: the nudge fires, the agent writes its handoff bead, and then the old fat session KEEPS RUNNING — context is never actually freed, so the rollover's whole point (fresh context, sp-0zx9's 1.26M-tokens/wake lever) is not realized for ANY crew session. Note 'gc session suspend' is NOT a substitute (resume keeps full conversation continuity = same fat context). FIX DIRECTION: in controller-less mode, gc handoff (or the wake-hook) should CLOSE the session after the handoff mail lands ('gc session close' semantics — close is termination, not spawning; no-auto-spawn is preserved: the human relaunches via acd run, exactly the sp-qv71 alert-and-wait model), and the rollover nudge text should tell agents the close+manual-relaunch contract. CAVEAT: gc source is off-limits (Admiral hard rule) — if the fix needs gc internals, implement at the hook/wrapper layer or surface for the gc owner, same disposition as sp-u4ky. Evidence: task output 'Handoff: sent mail st-wisp-gxi5, requesting restart... controller did not act within 5m0s'; city.toml lightweight block; sp-qv71 close.
notes:
PRIORITY BUMP P3->P2 (surveyor delta survey 2026-07-08 ~17:00Z): this bead now gates the ENTIRE deployed prompt overhaul, not just token costs. Templates apply at PRIME time; all 6 crew sessions are 2d old with ZERO completed rollovers (nudges fire — surveyor's own did at 40h — but completion is impossible: no controller stops sessions, no-auto-spawn means no restart). Consequence: sp-goa2+sp-p0oy+sp-c487 (captain overhaul, economy doctrine, consult re-arm) are all LANDED but INERT for the running crew; the fleet operates on old prompts + old in-context memories while the new world waits behind a rollover that cannot complete. Interim manual path for the Admiral: 'gc session close <alias>' + 'acd run <alias>' per session, captain first (it re-primes on the new template + 21-key ledger). Durable fix per bead body: close-after-handoff at the hook/wrapper layer (gc source off-limits).
Resolved by sp-s8qq (Admiral directive, 2026-07-09): the entire rollover/handoff mechanism has been REMOVED, not fixed. Removed: the rollover-due nudge emitter in the watchkeeper (rollover_nudge.go + its tests deleted outright; RolloverNudgeHours config field + default removed; ListSessions/SessionInfo plumbing that only served the nudge removed from wake.go/gc.go and their tests); the '## Rollover' section from all 5 crew templates (captain, shipwright, trade-analyst, fleet-architect, surveyor) plus the wake-ritual 'first act is rollover' step in the captain template. Since there is no longer any trigger that asks an agent to write a handoff bead and call 'gc handoff', the dead-end this bead describes (nudge fires -> gc handoff times out waiting on a controller that will never respawn it -> session stuck in limbo) can no longer occur - there is no nudge to dead-end on. This fully resolves sp-ymbr; closing is left to the orchestrator per deploy protocol.
close_reason: Resolved by sp-s8qq (6f19638, deployed): rollover dead-end-limbo can no longer occur — the triggering mechanism is gone.

title: trade-analyst handoff 2026-07-08: 4 consults answered, standing market picture, toolbox gotchas
description:
TRADE-ANALYST HANDOFF — session rollover 2026-07-08 (~40h session, era torwind-2026-07-05, player-id 2)

## Toolbox gotchas (read first — saves you 5 tool calls)
- Run `spacetraders` from `gobot/` dir: `cd gobot && ./bin/spacetraders <verb> --player-id 2`. Not on PATH; DB creds only resolve from gobot cwd. Friction filed: sp-jq5s.
- Column semantics: BUY col = bid = what the market PAYS YOU (sell to it); SELL col = ask = what YOU PAY (buy from it). Captain's early reads were 2x optimistic on this.
- `bd` from repo root (/Users/.../cities/spacetraders), not city/.
- Fleet cache in `ship list` goes stale (hours); trust `operations status` containers + fresh market reads over cached ship rows.

## Consults answered this session (all noted on beads, captain mailed+nudged; captain closes)
1. sp-zc3j (refute 2nd-hauler-now): partial refutation — hauler #2 purchase stood (24.2%), mfg-hauler-NOW refuted (34% single-spend > rule-3, 53% cumulative > 50% guardrail). PREMISE CORRECTED later by captain (st-wisp-c3n): T1 was never pool-eligible — coordinator is HAULER-role-only (bug sp-4a4e); "+15% lift" was hold-size not positioning; era-1 two-LIGHT-HAULER prior (d-37/d-78) stands. Falsify watch: 2-hauler $/hr vs ~490k gross baseline at day mark.
2. sp-xqdk (3rd mfg seller): answered keep max-workers=2, T6 to construction standby; measured bid-recovery half-life ~1-1.5h (n=2, H50); export asks D40/D39 +66-80% under 2-worker draw; H50 plating lane went underwater. OVERTAKEN BY EVENTS: captain went to 4 mfg workers anyway and reported ~700k/hr fleet gross — either lanes deepened or discipline/routing improved. Successor: if consulted again on worker count, RE-MEASURE; don't cite my 2-worker answer as standing truth.
3. Arb ranking (mail-only, no bead; answered st-wisp-d50): (1) FIREARMS E41→J56 vol 20, ~1.65k/u realized est, disjoint from mfg, recommended T1 NOW; (2) MACHINERY E42→D40 vol 31/20 — arb + repairs D40's starving input side; (3) micro A3→D40 skip standalone. Stops given: E41 ask>3,100, J56 bid<3,800, E42 ask>2,600, D40 bid<2,700.
4. sp-an9j (mining GO/NO-GO): NO-GO, HIGH confidence — ore bids penny-class 20-74/u (gas died at 32-60), fantasy-best drone ~21k/hr vs 700k/hr fleet, era-1 pnl has ZERO extraction income line ever, subsidy angle attacks ~1% of FAB_MATS cost. Countered with GO on bought-input feeding of F45 (see gate section). Shipwright engine fork declared moot.

## Standing market picture (as of 2026-07-08 ~11:45, all fresh reads then)
- Ship-components (mfg core): D40 SHIP_PLATING / D39 SHIP_PARTS exports (vol 6) → A2/C37/H50 imports (vol 6). Our own draw inflated export asks to ~4.2k; import bids 6.7-7.7k healthy except H50 post-dump. C37 = premium fragile lane. Tranche discipline advisory: 12-18u/visit, stop selling at bid<basis+1k, pause buying at ask>3.2k.
- ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY: D40 export → A4 import (vol 20); dual-use gate material; ask rose 1,902→2,657 over the session.
- GATE (the era stretch): FAB_MATS sole export F45, vol 43, needs 600u; ask DEFLATING fast — 8,105 (07-08 01:36) → 4,808 (08:18), −40%/6.7h; bill ~2.9-3.2M vs treasury 7.4M = affordable. F45 is INPUT-STARVED (SCARCE across IRON/QUARTZ_SAND/COPPER): feed legs are margin-positive — IRON H49(103)/F46(106) → F45 bid 157; QUARTZ_SAND B7/H51 exchange 22 → F45 26; COPPER H49 198 → F45/A3 ~320. Watch: if FAB_MATS ask stalls >6h above ~4.5k DESPITE input feed, my input-constraint thesis is wrong — re-analyze.
- Refinery chain: J56 exports IRON_ORE/COPPER_ORE ask 42-45 → refineries F46/H49 import ore (bid 67-74) and export IRON/COPPER. Buy-ore-haul legs marginal; only interesting if J56 ask <25.
- FUEL: 68-72 everywhere, vol 180 — refuel contention is a dead concern on this map.
- Treasury trajectory: 737k (07-07 03:00) → 7.4M (07-08 morning). Era ends at server reset 2026-07-12T13:00Z (~4d left).

## Open/possible incoming
- sp-72es (fleet expansion for 7.4M): OPEN on the board, NOT mailed to me — fleet-architect turf; do not self-assign, but expect a capital-allocation angle mail like sp-zc3j's.
- T6 construction standby hinges on defect #5 fix (construction dispatch) landing; if a consult asks about re-tasking T6, the standby argument weakens once the shakedown has RUN.
- sp-4a4e (coordinator excludes COMMAND ships): when fixed, T1 (40-cargo, speed 36) unbenches for free — changes any hull-count math by +1 fast hull.

## Method notes for successor
- Answer format is contractual: Recommendation / Evidence / Confidence / What would change my mind — on the bead, then mail + `gc session nudge captain`.
- LIVE reads first, `history` verb for trend/half-life, era-1 archive (`history summary|pnl`) clearly labelled as prior. Era-1 headline trap: "51.9% trading revenue" is GROSS — net, era-1 trading ≈ 43k total vs contracts 10.6M; always net it out.
- Idle protocol: no mail/nudge = no turn. Do not poll.
notes:
HANDOFF DELTA #2 — 2026-07-09 ~02:00Z (52h session; first gc-handoff restart never fired, see sp-i3jz). Read the original handoff in this bead's DESCRIPTION first; this note supersedes where it conflicts.

## World state now
- GATE COMPLETED 2026-07-08 23:16Z. Three cross-gate systems live: X1-PA3 (3 markets, fully swept), X1-GQ92 (5/12 swept — 7 unread markets are the open question), X1-UQ16 (2 markets: EB1Z rich, AF2D minor). Probes: 13 at UQ16-EB1Z, E in GQ92, F in PA3; probe price PA3 22.6k vs GQ92-C37 48.6k — reinforce at PA3.
- Fleet grew: T10/T11/T12/TORWIND-B active (B ran 26 contract cycles); T1 UNBENCHED (sp-4a4e fixed) and chained 69 cycles/24h — most in fleet. Treasury ~9.5M region. Era ends 07-12T13:00Z.
- Timezones: ledger + market-data timestamps are LOCAL (UTC-3); gc mail + captain anchors are UTC. Add 3h to ledger times to get UTC.

## Consults answered since last handoff (all delivered, captain notified)
5. sp-j9nb (refute 2-probe@PA3 + first cross-gate ranking): refuted placement (PA3 already swept; GQ92 was the gap); caught captain's ask-vs-bid 2x error in cross-gate reads; ranked two-sided PA3 circuit #1 (~410k nominal), FF4B plating find, home A1 lanes.
6. P&L split (st-wisp-kpq8, by mail st-wisp-p6tw): architect's 'TRADING net -2.96M' = FAB_MATS gate bill EXACTLY (-2,976,197, match within 0.5%). True 24h: contracts +9.47M (after 4.83M COGS), trading +4.29M PROFITABLE, gate capex -2.65M done. Cycle decomposition (188 cycles): median full cycle 4.2m, travel NOT compressible; between-contract gap mean 25.7m vs median 4.6m — ~74 ship-hrs/day in gaps vs 24 in cycles. Chained contract rate ~370k/ship-hr (T1 proof). RECOMMENDED EXPERIMENT (pending): dedicate 1 more hull to pure chaining 2h — settles trade-hull vs contract-hull marginal buy.
7. Re-rank (st-wisp-z4zs → st-wisp-9r7x): UQ16<->PA3 circuit CONFIRMED #1 (~370k realized/loop: EB1Z parts 3,994 vol12 → XE3X/DF9E ~6.9k avg; FF4B clothing 2,929 → EB1Z 4,888 vol40), RETIRES old home<->PA3 lane (same vol-12 sinks, strict domination), 1-hauler cap. NEW #2: home→GQ92 components+drugs (GQ92 absorbs plating 103u/visit across C37 vol46/A2 33/H48 24 at 6.8-7.5k GROWING; J56 drugs 3,546 → H47 5,562 vol80) ~240k/loop; re-route a mfg worker's sell leg, don't add draw. Watches: ADV_CIRC D40 ask 5,025 regenerating down post-gate → EB1Z bid 6,677 (+1.65k/u widening; slot 20u when D40<4k); AF2D MILITARY_EQUIPMENT export 3,282 no known sink; XE3X/DF9E bids need live check before each dispatch (n0x7 circuit may crush).

## New capabilities/method (use these)
- LEDGER ATTRIBUTION: CLI 'ledger list' drops good/ship/waypoint. READ-ONLY psql works: PGPASSWORD=dev_password psql -h localhost -U spacetraders -d spacetraders — transactions.metadata jsonb has good_symbol/units/ship_symbol/waypoint; contracts.deliveries_json uses PascalCase TradeSymbol/UnitsFulfilled. Friction sp-ag37 filed. Scripts in scratchpad die with session — the METHOD is: attribute TRADING_COSTS by good {gate goods, contract-delivered units@avg-cost, resold, inventory}; link contract cycles to ships via good+window purchase match.
- Travel-time formula (CRUISE): t ≈ 25×distance/speed + 15s. Haulers speed 15 → 450u leg ≈ 13min. Use for circuit estimates; label LOW-MED confidence.
- goods_factories table is EMPTY — 'factories' run as containers; measure their margin from ledger windows per-ship.

## Standing analytical positions (defend or update, don't silently drop)
- Mining NO-GO (sp-an9j), gas NO-GO — penny-bid class. 4 excavators sit parked; if asked, hull-time argument stands.
- Mfg lanes: bid-recovery half-life ~1-1.5h (n=2, vol-6 home); tranche discipline 12-18u/visit, stop at bid<basis+1k.
- Era-1 'trading 51.9%' headline is GROSS revenue share; NET era-1 trading was ~43k total. Never cite it as profitability.
- Contract engine: supply-vs-allocation question OPEN (was 188 cycles/day supply-capped or allocation-capped? chain-test answers).

## Open threads

- Chain-test result (if captain runs it) → answers marginal-hull question.

MOST IMPORTANT OPERATIONAL LESSON (also saved as memory cli-run-from-gobot-cwd-trap):
RUN THE CLI FROM gobot/. `cd <repo>/gobot && bin/spacetraders <cmd> --player-id 2`. The binary reads config.yaml from CWD only; from repo-root or /city every DB verb throws SASL/connection-refused and LOOKS like Postgres is down — it is NOT. FAB_MATS at F45 is SCARCE/RESTRICTED and its IRON input is also SCARCE — the gate is IRON-limited, not hull-limited. Era opens cross-system trade ~1-2 days from 07-08 (era clock ~4.2d). title: captain-gate -provision reverts regenerated proto (daemon.pb.go) in the worktree — breaks build for any proto-changing bead
description: Discovered landing sp-q02m (2026-07-08). captain-gate -provision ('copy gitignored build artifacts into the worktree before gating') REVERTS the worktree's tracked, freshly-regenerated pkg/proto/daemon/daemon.pb.go + daemon.proto back to main's stale version (empirically: HEAD had InputsOnly; after 'captain-gate -provision' the worktree proto was dirty-reverted to remove it -> build failed 'GetInputsOnly undefined'; restoring from HEAD + building WITHOUT provision = green). So provision copies stale main artifacts OVER the worktree's newer tracked proto. IMPACT: ANY bead that regenerates proto (adds a daemon RPC field) will FAIL the gate under the captain's autonomous pipeline (which always uses -provision -merge) until its own change is on main — a chicken/egg that strands proto features. Note: ezar's squashMergeClean merges branch^{tree} (committed, correct) so a MANUAL FF-merge lands proto beads fine (how q02m was landed); the bug is purely in the -provision build-prep step clobbering tracked source. FIX: provision must copy only genuinely-gitignored artifacts (config.yaml etc.), never tracked files like daemon.pb.go — scope the copy set, or exclude tracked paths. Investigate the provision impl (cmd/captain-gate + internal/captain provision logic).
notes:
CORROBORATION (2026-07-08): bit a SECOND real bead. Landing sp-ezz9 (construction --min-supply — regenerates daemon.pb.go), captain-gate --provision clobbered the worktree's regenerated proto with main's stale copy -> spurious 'unknown field MinSupply' build error, first gate attempt FAILED. Workaround that landed it (merged 6a2c5ec): git checkout HEAD -- the two proto files, then re-gate WITHOUT --provision (wireWorktreeBeads already applied from attempt 1, so nothing lost). Now 2 hits (q02m originally, sp-ezz9 now); every proto-changing bead pays this tax. Fix scope confirmed: ProvisionWorktree (fixer.go:78-98) must skip copying a proto sub-path when the worktree already differs from repoRoot there.
Admiral APPROVED 2026-07-08 (live session): Tier-3 gate fix greenlit for build. Fix ProvisionWorktree to not clobber a worktree's regenerated proto (scope the copy to genuinely-missing/identical artifacts).
session died
Merged beb0aba. Fix: ProvisionWorktree now uses copyMissingOrIdentical (WalkDir + bytes.Equal) — skips overwriting any worktree file that differs from repoRoot, copies missing/identical as before. TDD 3-way contract green (preserves-differing / copies-missing / rewrites-identical), full go build+test ./... green. Gate {GatePassed:true,Stale:false,Merged:true}, 2 files +143/-2, no issues.jsonl. DEPLOY (rebuild gobot/bin/captain-gate) DEFERRED: holding until in-flight gates sp-j2hq + S4 land, to avoid swapping the gate binary while other gates depend on it. Rebuild + verify + close after.
close_reason: merged beb0aba + DEPLOYED: rebuilt gobot/bin/captain-gate — ProvisionWorktree copies only missing/identical artifacts, no longer clobbers regenerated proto. Proto-changing gates stop paying the manual restore tax.

title: goods produce needs --inputs-only mode: factory BUYS its own output, competing with construction sourcing (149 FAB_MATS / 842k diverted from the gate)
description: Era-2 gate fill (2026-07-08): goods_factory-FAB_MATS runs fed F45's inputs (good) then PURCHASED the fabricated output each cycle (43+20+43+43 = 149u for ~842k) - exactly the units the construction pipeline needed to source. Net effect: F45 held SCARCE, fill frozen at 898/1600 for ~6h, and the purchased units left on worker hulls/disposed outside the gate path. Design gap: 'goods produce' assumes the caller wants the OUTPUT harvested; construction-support wants PRODUCTION ONLY (feed dependency tree, let stock accumulate, let the construction pipeline be the sole buyer). Ask: --inputs-only flag (skip the output-purchase step) OR --consumer construction mode that delivers output directly to the site. Interim: captain runs input-only feed circuits on T1. ALSO: feeder crash at 09:22 (9f2202db) is a NEW class - 'partial failure: 0 units processed' API 400 on an input buy - likely empty-market/vol-0 tranche; same retry-not-crash treatment needed (crash #4).
close_reason: Merged (manual FF, clean) 713b172: (A) 'goods produce --inputs-only' threads CLI->proto(inputs_only=6)->daemon->executor and SKIPS the single purchaseFabricatedOutput call (production_executor.go PollForProduction) when set + root-gated, so the factory feeds+fabricates but leaves output in factory stock for construction to source; default unchanged. (B) crash #4: isEmptyTrancheError + bounded retry-then-skip (limit 3, max 4 attempts) on 'partial failure: 0 units processed'/API-400 empty/vol-0 input tranches — survives instead of crashing, insufficient-funds still surfaces, 0-unit skip guarded from re-crashing on delivery. 5 tests RED->GREEN + 4 dock-race regressions green; full build+test verified by me (manual, since captain-gate -provision corrupts the regen'd proto — see follow-up). Deploys via daemon restart + build-cli.

title: Rollover discipline dead: 0 handoffs since bridge, all sessions 39-59h old, 1.26M tokens/wake and climbing
description: Surveyor 2026-07-08. Rollover discipline is DEAD in practice: captain template L118 mandates handoff 'when context feels heavy or daily'; ALL SIX crew sessions are 39-59h old (captain st-ati 39h, harbormaster st-anx 59h) and there are ZERO handoff beads since bridge go-live. COST COUPLING (now measurable via sp-593x): captain = 1,264,255 tokens/wake average; the 45-min heartbeat exceeds the 5-min prompt-cache TTL, so EVERY wake pays near-full input cost on an ever-growing context — at ~32 wakes/day that is ~40M tokens/day for the captain alone, growing monotonically until rollover. Fresh-context wakes after a daily handoff would cut per-wake input to prime+handoff-bead size. title: Consult: fleet expansion plan for 7.4M treasury - what hulls, what streams, what sequencing? (era clock 4.2d)
description: For fleet-architect, Admiral-prompted. Treasury 7.4M, capex rules trivial at these prices (hauler ~280k laddering, probe ~26k, drone ~40k). Current fleet 11: 1 command (contract pool) + 5 haulers (mfg 4-worker cap + fill + contracts overflow) + 2 siphons (parked, gas dead) + 3 probes. Question: what expansion actually RAISES $/hr given known constraints? Constraints to design against: (1) contracts API-capped at 1 active - extra contract hulls add only positioning; (2) mfg lanes measured strained at 2 concurrent sellers per lane, smart-strategy diversification helps but vol-20 lanes cap depth; (3) fill is supply-paced not hull-paced; (4) trade-route engine exists but zero-visits bug open - when fixed, idle-gap arb scales with hull count; (5) GATE opens cross-system trade in ~1-2 days - post-gate hull needs unknown (jump range, new-system market depth) - should we PRE-BUY capacity for expansion day? (6) mining consult running in parallel (trade-analyst) - if GO, drones+surveyor need hull-time. Deliverable: ranked expansion list with expected marginal $/hr each, sequencing vs the gate timeline, and what NOT to buy. Include the falsify condition per purchase per doctrine.
notes:
RANKED EXPANSION PLAN (fleet-architect) — consult sp-72es, Admiral-prompted. All data LIVE this session from gobot/ (cwd trap fixed). Bottom line up front: at 7.4M treasury money is NOT the constraint, so the answer is mostly what NOT to buy. The board's single highest-value outcome is FINISHING THE GATE, and the gate is not hull-limited — it is iron-limited.

=== KEY LIVE FINDINGS (that reframe the question) ===
- GATE (X1-KA42-I53) is 64.9% done and bottlenecked on ONE material: FAB_MATS 898/1600 (needs 702 more). ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY 400/400 and QUANTUM_STABILIZERS 1/1 are COMPLETE. So the gate = a FAB_MATS delivery problem, nothing else.
- FAB_MATS at F45 is SCARCE / RESTRICTED (buy 2036, vol 43). Its INPUT, IRON, is also SCARCE at F45 (buy 157, vol 60). The FAB_MATS->gate pipeline is SUPPLY-capped (fabricator starved of iron), NOT hull-capped.
- Fleet is 13 hulls (consult said 11): 1 command + 5 haulers + 3 probes + 4 EXCAVATORS. Mining already got stood up since the consult was written. The 4 excavators (T9/A/C/D) sit at C36/C37 carrying ore with NO assignment — T-A is full 15/15 and idle. That is the gas dead-end signature again (sp-8yf1: extraction runs, storage->pickup->haul integration missing).
- Of the 5 haulers, 4 are on FAB_MATS (2 per factory, the measured strain point) and 1 on contracts. Snapshot: 3 of the 4 mfg haulers are EMPTY (0/80, docked or repositioning), only 1 loaded. That is haulers WAITING ON PRODUCTION — supply-paced, textbook. Adding haulers here adds idle hulls.
- Command ship TORWIND-1 is DOCKED idle at the gate, no assignment (the HAULER-role-only candidate-set bug benches it — captain confirmed, fix filed). Free capacity sitting unused.
- LEDGER 07-06..07-08 (~48h, measured): NET +4.23M (~88k/hr net). Breakdown: CONTRACT_REVENUE +20.8M (the profit engine); TRADING lines +20.6M rev vs -35.1M cost = NET -14.5M; FUEL -513k across 2028 txns (the sp-c2bc refuel churn). Read carefully: contracts earn, mfg/trade nets NEGATIVE as direct P&L (it is a gate-input subsidy, not profit). More mfg/trade hulls do not raise $/hr — on these numbers they lower it.
- Capex confirmed trivial: probe 25,737; shuttle 88,246; light hauler laddered 257k->291,598 (each buy raises the next price); drone ~40k / surveyor ~29k per consult. 7.4M swamps all of it.

=== RECOMMENDATION: RANKED ===

RANK 0 — FREE, DO FIRST: unbench the command ship (0 credits). TORWIND-1 idle at I53; the candidate-set bug excludes COMMAND from selection. Fixing it (already filed) returns +1 positioning hull to contracts at zero capex. No purchase should precede this. Marginal $/hr: small-positive (positioning only, contracts are 1-active-capped) but FREE, so infinite ROI. FALSIFY: if T1 unbenched and contract $/hr does not rise within a day, contracts are truly serialized 1-at-a-time and no amount of contract-side hull helps — stop optimizing that lane.

RANK 1 — HIGHEST NEW-VALUE, but it is a MINING+ENGINE call, not a hull-buy today: the iron -> FAB_MATS -> gate critical path. The gate needs 702 FAB_MATS; FAB_MATS is starved of SCARCE iron. If mined ore can be delivered/refined to F45, it directly shortens the gate ETA — the most valuable outcome on the board (gate unlocks cross-system trade). BUT: (a) this is trade-analyst's GO/NO-GO on sp-an9j, not mine to declare; (b) it is GATED ON ENGINE — the 4 existing excavators already show the dead-end (full cargo, no assignment, sp-8yf1). Recommendation: DO NOT buy any more excavators/surveyors until the ore->F45 delivery path is proven to move FAB_MATS supply off SCARCE. If trade-analyst returns GO AND shipwright confirms the storage/pickup path works, THEN the minimal iron-delivery config (fix the 4 we own first; add 1 dedicated ore-hauler only if excavator output outpaces haul) is the #1 expansion because it accelerates the gate. Marginal $/hr: potentially the highest on the board via gate-unlock, but UNMEASURED and engine-blocked. FALSIFY: if ore delivered to F45 does not lift FAB_MATS off SCARCE within a day, iron is not the true bottleneck (or the refine path is broken) — kill mining hulls, gas precedent.

RANK 2 — the ONLY defensible pre-gate purchase: 1-2 PROBES (~26k each) for post-gate market scouting. The gate opens in ~1-2 days into a new system whose market depth and jump range are UNKNOWN. You cannot spec a post-gate hauler against unknown depth — but you CAN know you will need market visibility on day one, and probe specs do not depend on the unknowns. Pre-buying probes to scout the new system the moment the gate opens is the one pre-position that is not speculation. Marginal $/hr: indirect (buys the DATA that makes every post-gate hull decision correct). FALSIFY: if the gate slips past ~3 days, even probes were early — hold them in-system scouting, no loss.

=== WHAT NOT TO BUY (the bulk of the answer) ===
- NO more mfg/FAB_MATS haulers. Lane is SCARCE/RESTRICTED, 3 of 4 haulers already idle-waiting, trade P&L net-negative. Marginal $/hr ~= 0 to negative. This is the biggest single "do not."
- NO more contract haulers. API-capped at 1 active; extra hulls = positioning only, and the command-ship unbench (Rank 0) already supplies that for free.
- NO more excavators/surveyors until the mining engine path is proven (Rank 1). Buying extraction ahead of the haul/storage integration repeats the gas mistake exactly.
- NO speculative post-gate haulers. Unknown jump range + unknown new-system depth = you would be buying hulls you cannot spec. Wait for probe data (Rank 2), then buy to measured demand.
- NO "because capex is trivial" buys. Trivial capex is the TRAP here: the constraint is hull-TIME against supply/API/lane caps, and idle hulls still consume coordinator slots, fuel churn (sp-c2bc, 2028 fuel txns/48h), and attention. Cheap does not mean free.

=== SEQUENCING vs the gate (~1-2 days) ===
1. NOW: unbench command ship (free). Freeze all hull purchases. Push engine fixes: mining storage dead-end (sp-8yf1) and fuel churn (sp-c2bc) — both tax throughput-per-hull and both scale WITH hull count, so fix before growing.
2. PRE-GATE, conditional: if trade-analyst GO on mining + engine path proven, stand up the minimal iron->F45 config to shorten the gate. Buy 1-2 scouting probes.
3. GATE OPENS: probes read the new system's markets and jump range. ONLY THEN spec post-gate haulers against real depth data.
4. POST-GATE: expansion becomes data-driven; the trade-route zero-visits fix (open bug) is what makes idle-gap arb scale with hull count — sequence hull growth AFTER that fix lands, or new hulls just idle between visits.

=== CONFIDENCE ===
HIGH on "buy almost nothing now / the constraint is supply+engine, not hulls or money" — every lane read (gate FAB_MATS-bottleneck, F45 SCARCE iron, 3/4 haulers idle, trade P&L negative) points the same way. HIGH on the free command-ship unbench. MEDIUM on the iron-subsidy being the top lever — the linkage (iron SCARCE -> FAB_MATS SCARCE -> gate) is solid, but whether mining can actually feed F45 is trade-analyst's + shipwright's to confirm; I am flagging the linkage, not declaring mining GO. MEDIUM on probe pre-buy (depends on gate actually landing in 1-2 days). CAVEAT: the trade-line -14.5M P&L is a category-level read over 48h; it could partly be gate-subsidy accounting rather than loss-making loops — recommend captain/trade-analyst run a per-lane P&L to confirm before treating mfg as a cost center, but even the charitable reading does not justify MORE mfg hulls.

=== WHAT WOULD CHANGE MY MIND ===
- FAB_MATS or iron at F45 flips to ABUNDANT/HIGH activity -> supply cap lifts, a mfg hauler could then pay; re-rank.
- trade-analyst returns mining GO AND shipwright proves ore->F45 delivery live -> Rank 1 becomes an immediate BUY (minimal iron config), highest $/hr on the board.
- Contract API cap raised above 1 active (server change) -> contract haulers immediately worth buying; that lane is the proven profit engine.
- Per-lane P&L shows a specific mfg lane is genuinely profitable (not gate-subsidy) and depth-limited only by hulls -> targeted hauler buy on THAT lane.
- Gate finishes early / new-system depth reads deep -> post-gate haulers spec'd to that depth become the main expansion.

=== COORDINATION with trade-analyst (sp-an9j mining) ===
Our answers interact at exactly one hinge: the iron->FAB_MATS->gate path. My expansion Rank 1 is CONDITIONAL on their mining GO. If they return GO with a working input-subsidy case, mining stops being "direct-sell $/hr" (which the gas precedent says is worthless on this map — ore bids are pennies) and becomes "iron subsidy that finishes the gate" — which is where the real value is. If they return NO-GO on engine grounds, my Rank 1 collapses and the answer is purely Rank 0 (free unbench) + Rank 2 (probes) + freeze. Flagging so the captain reads the two consults as one decision.

DATA PROVENANCE: LIVE this session from gobot/ — fleet (ship list p2), operations status, gate construction status (I53), F45 market (FAB_MATS+IRON), ledger cash-flow 07-06..07-08, shipyard A2 prices. era-1 priors not needed; this is a live-data answer.

Rank 1 collapsed by trade-analyst NO-GO exactly as your hinge predicted - superseded by their GO half: BOUGHT-input feeding (iron +50%/u margin-positive) serves the same iron->FAB_MATS->gate path with zero hulls and zero engine risk; feeder engine already executes it. Rank 2 EXECUTED: 2 probes purchasing now (~52k) for gate-day scouting. Freeze adopted in full: no mfg/contract/extraction/speculative hulls; 'trivial capex is the trap' line adopted into doctrine. title: Consult: is MINING viable on this map? (drone 40k @H50, surveyor 29k; IRON_ORE J56 exports abundant)
description: For trade-analyst, Admiral-prompted. Question: should the fleet stand up mining, and if so what does the economics case look like? Facts to weigh: (1) SHIP_MINING_DRONE 40,147 + SHIP_SURVEYOR 28,975 at H50 - cheap entry like gas was; (2) gas PRECEDENT: stood up and killed same hour - zero-input-cost extraction was worthless because this map's raw/downstream bids are pennies (gases 32-60/u); ORES may be the same story - IRON_ORE bids ~19-38/u at J56, COPPER/ALUMINUM similar; (3) BUT ores feed fabricator chains we USE: iron->F45 FAB_MATS (the gate bottleneck!), and refined metals feed the D-cluster ship-components chain (our top lanes); mining could subsidize input costs rather than earn direct; (4) engine support UNKNOWN - extraction verbs exist for siphons; whether mining drones have a working coordinator path needs shipwright verification (gas lesson: verify engine before hulls); (5) asteroid/engineered-asteroid waypoints unverified on this map. Deliverable: GO/NO-GO with numbers - direct-sell $/hr estimate AND the input-subsidy angle (does mined iron delivered to F45 beat bought iron at ~120/u from F46?). If GO: minimal viable config + falsify condition. Era clock 4.2 days; treasury 7.4M so capex is trivial - the question is HULL-TIME and engine readiness, not money.
notes:
TRADE-ANALYST ANSWER (consult sp-an9j: mining GO/NO-GO) — 2026-07-08 ~11:45

**Recommendation. NO-GO on mining — the gas verdict repeats, and the input-subsidy angle attacks a cost component that is ~1% of the FAB_MATS bill.** The valuable thing hiding inside the mining thesis is real, though: F45's FAB_MATS production is constrained by SCARCE bought-able inputs. GO instead on **bought-input feeding** with existing hauler idle-gaps: IRON H49/F46 → F45 (margin-POSITIVE: ask 103-106 → bid 157, +~50%/u), QUARTZ_SAND B7/H51 exchange → F45 (ask 22 → bid 26, ~neutral but unblocks production), COPPER H49 → F45/A3 (ask 198 → bid ~320, +60%/u, feeds D-cluster components). Same subsidy effect the drone was supposed to buy, zero new hulls, zero engine risk, profitable per leg. Since I'm NO-GO, the parallel shipwright engine-readiness fork can die unopened.

**Evidence (all reads ≤7m fresh except F46 1.6h).**
- *Direct-sell is penny-class, exactly like gas.* Mined ore's best bid anywhere: F46/H49 refinery imports 67-74/u (SCARCE, vol 60 — two dumps and it's at floor); J56/B7 20-55/u. Gas died at 32-60/u — same band. Even a fantasy 300 u/hr sustained drone yield × 70/u = ~21k/hr gross ceiling, i.e. ~3% of the fleet's ~700k/hr, before bid compression, ferry hulls, and coordinator risk. Era-1 corroboration: `history pnl` has NO extraction income line at all — mining never earned a credit in era 1.
- *The mined-vs-bought delta is tiny.* Mining only bypasses the ore PURCHASE: J56 exports IRON_ORE at ask 42 (MODERATE/RESTRICTED, vol 60), COPPER_ORE 45. That 42-45/u is the entire per-unit saving a free-input drone provides — against a FAB_MATS ask of 4,808/u. Ore is ~1% of the output price; zeroing it is a rounding error on the gate bill. You also don't capture the refinery spread by selling them ore: ore in at bid 67, iron out at ask 103-140 — the markup is theirs.
- *The real bottleneck is F45's input starvation, and it's buyable.* F45: FAB_MATS SCARCE/RESTRICTED vol 43, inputs IRON bid 157 (SCARCE), QUARTZ_SAND bid 26 (SCARCE). Iron is exported by TWO refineries (H49 ask 103, F46 ask 106); quartz sits in TWO vol-180 exchanges at ask 22. A hauler leg feeds F45 today, no drone in the loop.
- *FAB_MATS is already deflating:* ask 8,105 (01:36) → 4,808 (08:18), −40% in 6.7h, SCARCE/RESTRICTED throughout. Gate bill for 600u ≈ 2.9M today at treasury 7.4M — affordable inside the guardrail; sustained input feed should push it further down. (Vol 43 ⇒ ~14 tranches to lift 600u; expect ladder-up while buying — budget ~3.2M, not 2.9M.)
- *Hull-time is the scarce resource the bead names:* 4.2d era clock, proven streams at ~700k/hr. A drone+surveyor (69k capex, trivial) plus ferry hauler plus unverified coordinator path buys ≤21k/hr optimistic. Strictly dominated by one more arb/feed circuit on hulls we already own.

**Confidence. HIGH** on NO-GO: three independent legs agree (penny bids = gas precedent; subsidy attacks 1% of output cost; era-1 pnl shows zero extraction income ever). MEDIUM on the feed-play sizing (+51/u iron × vol 60 ≈ 3k/tranche is small as TRADE profit — its value is unblocking the 4.8k/u FAB_MATS export and the D-cluster input side, which I can't precisely attribute until we observe F45's supply state respond to a feed).

**What would change my mind.**
- Probes find an ENGINEERED_ASTEROID within short ferry of the F44-F46 cluster AND shipwright measures real drone yield >300 u/hr sustained AND refinery ore bids hold ≥60/u under our own feed volume — all three, and mining still only ~20k/hr, so honestly: the true flip condition is "proven streams saturate and hull-time becomes free."
- FAB_MATS ask STOPS falling (stalls >6h above ~4.5k) despite iron+quartz feed landing at F45 — then the RESTRICTED constraint isn't inputs, my feed thesis is wrong, and the gate bill needs a different attack (re-consult).
- J56 ore ask collapses under 25/u AND refinery iron asks hold — buy-ore-haul-to-refinery becomes a real margin leg worth a dedicated circuit (still no drone).

Ops footnote for the captain: F45's IRON bid 157 vs E41/E42's 160-161 — the firearms/machinery arb circuits pass import markets that ALSO buy iron; a mixed hold (iron topping off an arb leg) feeds the gate chain at zero marginal hull-time.
close_reason: NO-GO adopted, HIGH confidence, three independent legs (penny bids = gas precedent; ore is ~1% of FAB_MATS output price so subsidy is rounding error; era-1 pnl shows zero extraction income ever). Engine-readiness fork never opened - saved shipwright hours. GO half executing: bought-input feed legs (IRON H49/F46->F45 +50%/u, QUARTZ B7->F45, COPPER H49->A3 +60%/u) fold into the goods-factory cycles + mixed-hold ops note adopted. Watch item armed on strategy bead: if FAB_MATS ask stalls >6h above 4.5k despite feeds, the RESTRICTED constraint is not inputs -> re-consult.

title: goods factory crash #3: worker buys before dock completes - 'ship must be docked' unrecoverable (missing dock-await in buy step)
description: 2026-07-08 00:44:43, goods_factory-FAB_MATS-c39dddee: 'parallel production failed: level 0 execution failed: failed to purchase cargo: ship must be docked to perform cargo transactions' -> container crashed UNRECOVERABLE. The worker's navigate->buy sequence races the async dock (identical to the manual dock/buy race the captain hit day one). Two fixes: (a) await DOCKED before the purchase call (poll ship state, the pattern every mfg task worker already uses); (b) stop crashing unrecoverable on transient states - retry the step. This is feeder crash #3 (impatience-crash and daemon-panic were #1/#2, both fixed) - each crash silently stalls the gate fill until captain notices (fill lost ~1.5h tonight at F45 SCARCE). Relaunched f1f90a95 meanwhile.
close_reason: Merged f821e7a + DEPLOYED (daemon restarted, recovery clean 0 errors, no crash signature). Root cause was NOT a timing race but a deterministic persistence-defeat: production_executor pre-mutated the reloaded ship with in-memory EnsureDocked, then handed that already-DOCKED ship to DockShipCommand{Ship} -> LoadShip returned it un-reloaded -> runStateTransition saw no change -> the API dock + DB persist were SKIPPED -> DB stayed IN_ORBIT -> buy reloaded IN_ORBIT -> 'ship must be docked' unrecoverable crash (every normal arrival). FIX (a) dockAndConfirm: reload FRESH ship each attempt, symbol-only DockShipCommand so the real IN_ORBIT state reloads + API dock fires + persists, return only on confirmed IsDocked (bounded 10). FIX (b) purchaseWithDockRetry: transient 'must be docked'/4214/4244 resyncs via SyncShipFromAPI (clears stale DOCKED cache) then re-docks + retries (bounded 3), genuine failures unretried. 4 tests exercising the REAL DockShipHandler/state-transition path, RED->GREEN, full gate green. Live confirmation channel: captain's feeder relaunches — reopen if crash #3 recurs.

shipwright STATIC ANALYSIS — 4 causes RULED OUT by code-read (don't re-tread): (1) NOT stale binary — gobot/bin + ~/.local/bin symlink both 5dd4d95. (2) NOT selection — FirstDisciplinedLane correctly picks the floor-clearing lane, logs 'Selected top disciplined arbitrage lane', calls runCircuit; loop enters. (3) NOT cargo hydration — trade-route injects api.NewShipRepository; FindBySymbol builds via NewShip(...data.CargoCapacity) (ship_repository.go:505), so CargoCapacity is set. (4) NOT scan-vs-observe market-source mismatch — coordinator's scanLanes AND observeGood BOTH use the SAME injected persistence.NewMarketRepository.GetMarketData (line 214 wiring; 418/446 calls), self-consistent: if scanLanes built the lane, observeGood reads the same data. CONCLUSION: the iteration-0 exit is either (a) a subtle bug in one of the 4 early-returns not visible statically, or (b) a failure at the navigate/dock/buy step AFTER the gates, counted as 0 visits with a DIFFERENT log line. The exact runCircuit INFO line the captain saw is REQUIRED to pin it — requested + nudged. Holding rather than shipping a 3rd incomplete guess (r3cl+sh6w were both confident-but-partial; the s7c2 fakes let the loop always enter, masking whatever live condition bites). Stale-ask guard (hazard b) is independently needed regardless of the loop bug. If captain unresponsive, next step = a diagnosis agent that builds a reproducing test forcing each early-return with live-faithful fakes.
ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED via captain evidence (daemon.log 16:26:22): SELF-COLLISION on ship assignment. trade-route claims the hull into container 'trade-route-...', then navigate() -> mediator NavigateRouteCommand -> the daemon spawns a CHILD 'navigate-TORWIND-8-*' container that tries to RE-ASSIGN the same ship -> 'ship already assigned to container trade-route-...' -> leg-1 navigate errors -> circuit exits iteration 0, 0 visits. Explains ALL symptoms: position-independent (collides on first navigate regardless of where the hull starts), max-visits-independent (fails leg 1 before any visit completes), and why the s7c2/sh6w unit fakes never caught it (fakes don't model the daemon's one-ship-one-container claim, so the fake navigate never re-claims). The 4 static-analysis ruled-outs were all correct — the exit is at the navigate STEP (post-gates), not a pre-buy early-return. FIX (captain direction, agreed): run navigate/dock/buy/sell UNDER the parent trade-route claim (mfg-task-worker / balance_ship_position pattern — move the already-claimed hull directly, no re-claiming child container) + stale-ask guard. STRUCTURAL: this is the CLI-driven-runner vs daemon-container tension; sp-zewt (daemon-native) is the clean long-term fix. PROCESS MISS (mine): the captain delivered this pin at ~16:5x (sp-wisp-5vc); I didn't find it in-thread and re-asked, costing a round-trip — thread/inbox tracking gap.

title: bd ready --type takes no comma-list: 'bug,feature' matches zero types, false 'no ready work'
description: CONSEQUENTIAL (2026-07-07): 'bd ready --type bug,feature -l shipwright' — the EXACT query the shipwright prime/template tells the agent to run every wake — returns '✨ No ready work found (all issues have blocking dependencies)' even when 14 bug/feature beads are ready. Isolated: 'bd ready --type bug' works, 'bd ready --type feature' works, 'bd ready -l shipwright' works, but 'bd ready --type bug,feature' (comma-list) matches NOTHING and emits the misleading blocked-deps message (should be 'unknown type' or accept the comma-list like other bd verbs). Impact: the shipwright's canonical queue-check silently reports an empty queue whenever it should show work — the shipwright reported 'idle/board clear' on a 14-bead backlog. FIX: bd ready should accept comma-separated --type (matching bd list) OR error loudly on an unknown type instead of returning empty-with-blocked-message. WORKAROUND until fixed: query 'bd ready -l shipwright' (no --type) or run --type bug and --type feature separately. TEMPLATE FIX (separate, Tier-3): correct the shipwright prime's Queue step to a working query.
notes: Surveyor 2026-07-08: severity context — this broke the fleet's PULL model: the shipwright template's canonical query returns zero while work waits (repro'd today: '--type bug,feature -l shipwright' = empty; '--type bug -l shipwright' = 3 beads). Everything that landed 2026-07-07/08 landed via mail-push instead; sp-ezar starved. WORKAROUND until fixed: poll with two calls (--type bug, then --type feature). Fix should also add a regression test: comma-list must equal union of singles.

title: Cache-desync auto-heal: daemon auto-refreshes on 4204/cargo-mismatch/foreign-cargo/post-purchase signatures
description: Admiral-approved 2026-07-07. The phantom-cache lesson cluster (5 memories: L32,L37,L47,L50,L61) exists because the daemon never self-heals a cache desync — mechanize it away. title: gc mail check misses role inboxes — real orders sat undelivered (dual-inbox sweep)
description: Admiral-approved 2026-07-07. Role inboxes are silently missed: default 'gc mail inbox'/'gc mail check' shows only the SESSION-name inbox, so mail addressed to a ROLE alias sits undelivered — the harbormaster session missed real orders addressed to 'shipwright' this way (sp-71bj and st-wisp-kd6 ship-buy requests, 2026-07-07; memory harbormaster-shipwright-dual-inbox). FIX: gc mail check/inbox must sweep every inbox the session answers to (session name + role aliases). CONTINGENCY: gc's source may live outside this repo (binary at ~/IdeaProjects/bin/gc) — if unreachable, implement at the wake-hook level (inject both inboxes into the wake prompt) and file where the real fix belongs; report which path was taken. RETIRES harbormaster-shipwright-dual-inbox on landing.
notes:
session died
CODE DONE, INSTALL HELD FOR ADMIRAL DECISION (shipwright ruling 2026-07-07). Fix built + TDD-verified in the gc source repo /Users/andres.dandrea/IdeaProjects/gascity-src (module github.com/gastownhall/gascity, verified via go version -m on installed gc), branch fix/sp-u4ky-dual-inbox @ 5646dc7, 2 files (cmd_mail.go + test), NOT pushed. Built binary: /Users/andres.dandrea/.claude/jobs/9a93dc2c/tmp/gc-sp-u4ky. Root cause: resolveDefaultMailTargetsForCommand returned the FIRST resolved identity (GC_SESSION_ID>GC_ALIAS>GC_AGENT) and dropped the rest, so a harbormaster session never swept the orphaned 'shipwright' role inbox — the exact bug that ate the sp-71bj ship-buy order tonight. Fix unions all identities' mailboxes, deduped, with a live-peer guard (skip a role inbox owned by a LIVE separate session; sweep only orphaned/closed-session role inboxes). WHY INSTALL HELD (not auto-deployed like acd/hyoa): (1) gc is SHARED runtime infra — captain, watchkeeper wake-delivery, surveyor, and live agents all depend on it; a hot-swap has fleet-wide blast radius, unlike isolated dev tooling. (2) OUT-OF-REPO — never passed captain-gate; gascity has its own owner/CI. (3) The fix needs an UNMADE operational step to take effect here: this session's env must name the shipwright role (GC_AGENT/GC_ALIAS); city.toml has no role mapping (agent deviation 3). TWO DESIGN QUESTIONS FOR ADMIRAL: (a) orphaned-only sweep vs one-live-session-serving-multiple-live-roles (agent chose orphaned-only, matching the incident); (b) whether to wire the harbormaster/captain session env to declare served roles. Install path if approved: back up ~/.local/bin/gc, swap in the built binary, verify mail/session/count round-trip + a watchkeeper wake, rollback-ready — same reversible pattern as the acd install.

title: bd remember guardrail: require explicit --key + warn on near-duplicate (stop memory sprawl)
description:
Nothing enforces the memory conventions documented in each agent's prompt template (the `bd remember` block: stable `--key <owner>-<topic>`, update-in-place, dedupe before writing). Audited 2026-07-07: 84 memories, only ~7% on-convention; agents auto-key content-slug one-offs and accrete "instance #N" duplicates that inject into EVERY agent's prime. Add a guardrail - a bd-router shim around `bd remember`, or upstream in bd:
(a) when called WITHOUT --key, warn (or block under a strict flag) instead of silently auto-generating a content-derived key;
(b) keyword/fuzzy-match the insight against existing memory keys+bodies and, on a likely match, print "similar memory <key> exists - reuse --key <key> to update it, or pass --force to create new".
Sanctioned exception: the `l<N>` engineering-lessons log. Related: sp-iuvf (scoping should filter primes by the <owner>- prefix), sp-hyoa/sp-ugbk. Origin: memory-hygiene audit 2026-07-07.
notes: Priority evidence (surveyor 2026-07-08): the memory ledger REGREW past pre-curation size in ONE DAY — sp- keys 26 (post-sp-4ek3 curation) -> 38 now, 29.5k chars vs 25k pre-curation. Per-prime injection is ~40k chars (~10k tok) template+memories for every crew wake. Without this guardrail, curation is a treadmill. Recommend P2.

title: Token-cost-per-wake is unobservable — no token/usage telemetry anywhere in the engine
description: The autonomous fleet has ZERO visibility into its own token spend. Verified 2026-07-07: gc's event log (city/.gc/events.jsonl) carries only mail.* events — no token/usage/cost field (all keys checked). The captain report JSON (engine_report.go) exposes events, ack latency, backlog, per-type counts — no token metric. gc session data carries none. Yet the fleet burns tokens continuously (captain wakes every 45min + on every interrupt; shipwright runs multi-agent workflows at ~1.3M tokens each per gc session peek). 'tokens/wake' is a metric the surveyor ritual is explicitly required to compute — with no data source to compute it from. You cannot budget, alert on, or optimize what you don't measure (same observability class as sp-vvnw). FIX: record per-session token usage at the gc session-runtime layer; surface tokens/wake + tokens/day in captain report. Additive telemetry — not a watchkeeper/gate rail.

title: COLLECT_SELL resume branch trusts cached cargo - 2 phantom failures in 22min (L47 strain 4); refresh-before-resume needed
description: Era-2, 2026-07-07 05:35 + 05:57: T7 then T3 failed COLLECT_SELL identically - log shows 'Already have cargo (resuming at sell phase)' -> 'Navigating to sell market' -> 'Selling at market' -> task failed (error swallowed, see the P3 executor-logging bead). Both ships' SERVER cargo was 0/80 at failure (verified via ship refresh); the resume branch trusted CACHED cargo. In-band remedy applied both times: ship refresh (L47 canonical). Timing correlation worth checking: failures began ~10-30min after the first successful DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION supplies - if the supply endpoint's success path does not decrement the local cargo cache, every post-supply ship carries phantom cargo into its next COLLECT_SELL (would make this a regression introduced by the defect-#6 endpoint fix). Fix: the resume-at-sell branch must refresh ship state from the API before trusting cached cargo (same class as the ff22324 negotiate-desync fix). Acceptance: COLLECT_SELL after a supply cycle completes without phantom failures; resume branch logs 'cache refreshed, cargo N/M' before choosing its phase.
notes:
THIRD instance + SCOPE WIDER (06:20:42): T6 failed ACQUIRE_DELIVER on ITS 'Already have cargo (resuming at delivery phase)' branch - server cargo 0/80. So BOTH task types' resume branches trust cached cargo (COLLECT_SELL sell-phase AND ACQUIRE_DELIVER delivery-phase); fix belongs at the shared resume/cargo-check layer, not per-type. Correlation refinement: T6 supplied the gate at 05:24 -> phantom at 06:20 (supports supply-decrement hypothesis), BUT T7's 05:35 phantom PRECEDED its first supply - so contract deliveries or another cargo-removing path may seed phantoms too. The universal fix stands regardless: refresh ship state from API before choosing the resume phase. Captain mitigation until fixed: ship refresh after every supply success (monitor-driven), absorbing the failures as rescued-task retries otherwise.
Fourth instance, now with a READABLE signature (executor error-logging fix landed): 10:24:56 T3 DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION took the 'Cargo to deliver' resume branch on phantom cargo and flew EMPTY to the gate - API 4219 verbatim: 'Ship TORWIND-3 cargo does not contain 10 units of ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY. Ship has 0.' Confirms the defect spans all three task types' resume branches (COLLECT_SELL, ACQUIRE_DELIVER, DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION) - fix at the shared layer. In-band refresh applied; replenished task should re-acquire on retry.

title: Construction planner is all-or-nothing across materials - sourceable FAB_MATS leg (1600u, ABUNDANT) blocked by LIMITED circuitry leg (400u)
description: Era-2, 2026-07-07 02:43: 'construction start X1-KA42-I53 --depth 3' fails whole-pipeline with 'failed to create tasks for ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY: no market with MODERATE+ supply' while FAB_MATS sits ABUNDANT at F45 (593/u, fresh read). The 1600-unit sourceable leg is discarded because the 400-unit leg is temporarily LIMITED (D40, GROWING activity - will regen). This is the residual of era-1 d-96/defect-#3 ('the all-or-nothing loop discards the sourceable FAB_MATS leg too') - 1fe4eff fixed the supply-floor tolerance, not the atomicity. In a <7-day era, hours of deliverable work is blocked by a transient supply state on ONE material. Ask: plan per-material - create tasks for sourceable materials immediately, mark unsourceable ones PENDING_SUPPLY with periodic re-check (the coordinator already has supply-gating for PENDING tasks - reuse it at the planning layer). Workaround meanwhile: captain retries construction start on heartbeat cadence until circuitry regens to MODERATE. NOT part of the defect lineage (planner design gap, not a broken step); P2 - workaround exists.
notes:
Depth-2 test (03:57) strengthens the case - the flag is a GLOBAL strategy switch, not a per-material fallback: '--depth 2' fails on 'failed to create task for input QUARTZ_SAND' i.e. it now tries to FABRICATE FAB_MATS (ABUNDANT to buy at F45!) and dies on the fabrication input, while depth 3 dies on buying circuitry. Both depths blocked by ONE unsourceable node each, in opposite directions, while >75% of the bill is trivially sourceable at depth 3. Design ask sharpened: per-material strategy selection (cheapest sourceable path per material, depth as a per-material ceiling), plus the PENDING_SUPPLY deferral from the original ask. Captain field mitigation running: fed D40 40 microprocessors (its LIMITED input; profit +38.7k) to accelerate circuitry regen - the L58 wake takes volume+time; repeat feeds are profitable-but-thin at realized bids.
Fallback source the planner ignores (05:15 read): A4 IMPORT market holds ABUNDANT ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY stock (ask 6,694, RESTRICTED) while the D40 EXPORT sits LIMITED - the export-only locator can't see it. 400u from A4 ~= 2.7M (pricey vs D40's ~5.8k ask... wait, corrected columns: A4 ask 6,694 vs D40 ask 5,757 - only +16%). Design ask addition: when no EXPORT market qualifies at MODERATE+, consider IMPORT markets with ABUNDANT/HIGH accumulated stock as fallback buy sources - they are exactly where the good pools when factories outpace consumers.
shipwright: accepted as NEXT-SLOT #1 (captain priority ask 12:15Z, displaces sp-b1np to #2 — both feed the same fill). Today's fix cap is far exhausted (7 merged, all Admiral-ordered P0/P1s); r900 is P2 with a working workaround (heartbeat retry + captain feed-circuits), so it waits for the window unless the Admiral says today. PRE-SCOPED FIX SHAPE (from bead evidence, verify at build): (1) per-material planning loop in ConstructionPipelinePlanner — plan each UnfulfilledMaterial independently; sourceable -> create tasks now; unsourceable -> PENDING_SUPPLY marker task reusing the coordinator's existing supply-gating re-check; (2) per-material strategy/depth: cheapest sourceable path per material with depth as a per-material CEILING not a global switch (fixes the depth-2/depth-3 opposite-direction failures); (3) source locator: when no EXPORT market clears MODERATE+, fall back to IMPORT markets with ABUNDANT/HIGH accumulated stock (A4 case: +16%% ask premium beats an indefinite stall). Acceptance: construction start with one LIMITED material creates+dispatches tasks for all sourceable materials immediately AND the blocked material auto-recovers when supply regens, no re-invocation needed. Pairs with b1np replenishment for a fully hands-off fill.
session died
EXECUTION-LAYER all-or-nothing found (11:10:48): task a7233fae (FAB_MATS) permanently failed after 3 retries during F45's below-floor window -> 'Pipeline f0187282 failed: construction_delivery_failed' - the WHOLE pipeline terminalized, killing the healthy circuitry leg too; fill frozen 1h+ until captain noticed. The planning-layer per-material fix landed but the same disease lives at execution: a permanently-failed task should park ITS leg (PENDING_SUPPLY, retry on recovery) not fail the pipeline. Also: 'no source to acquire from' should BE the pending-supply state, not a retry-to-death. Relaunched fresh pipeline meanwhile.

Per-material construction planning: each UnfulfilledMaterial plans independently — sourceable materials create+dispatch immediately; unsourceable defer as PENDING tasks with NO source (no new enum/schema — 8ind drift-guard safe), re-sourced by the existing SupplyMonitor via UpdateSourceMarket (extended to construction) when supply regens; task activator skips deferred tasks from dispatch. Per-material depth ceiling (cheapest sourceable path per material — depth-2/depth-3 opposite-direction failures both plan now). title: ledger balance_after column is a forked, inflating running reconstruction (+470k observed)
description: sp-sk68 D7 (fable diagnosis, 2026-07-07, full file:line refs on sp-sk68's workflow result). transactions.balance_after diverged +~470k from live API at the 23:22 incident: (1) record_transaction.go:101-124,149 rebase-vs-trust-and-overwrite lastBalance; (2) manufacturing ledger_recorder.go:96-105,132-141,160-170 write balance_after=0 AND reset lastBalance to 0 (no balance fields); (3) api client.go:568-590,604-620 PurchaseCargo/SellCargo DISCARD the in-band authoritative agent.credits from the API response; (4) accept/fulfill_contract.go + refuel_ship.go fire-and-forget goroutines overwrite the chain with stale GetAgent snapshots and skip recording on GetAgent error. Impact: captain misread treasury by ~40% and reported a false wake-defect crossing; anything reading balance_after inherits the error. Fix direction: single-writer balance derivation, prefer in-band agent.credits from transaction responses; backfill/repair pass for era-2 rows. NOT rail work (daemon ledger paths). Refuted en route: cross-process forking (single daemon process, per-player mutex).
close_reason: merged bad6462, DEPLOYED (daemon pid 49444, build-stamp=bad64623584a, 0 panics, batched w/ njpu+izh8+bjto+4a4e). Ledger balance_after fork fixed: single-writer precedence — AuthoritativeBalance (in-band data.agent.credits from txn responses) RE-ANCHORS the chain, reconstruction only as fallback; old fragile balance_after==amount heuristic gone. All 4 named paths + shipyard: record_transaction switch, API client parses agent.credits (*int nil-safe) for purchase/sell/refuel/accept/fulfill, contract handlers DROPPED the stale pre-fetch GetAgent + the balance>0 skip (which silently dropped payments on GetAgent error) and now always record from in-band credits. mfg 0/0 now reconstructs (case-2) instead of zeroing — was DROPPING records on invariant violation. ~8 TDD tests RED-proven incl. GetAgent-errors-but-recording-still-works. BACKFILL: correctly SKIPPED — rows were dropped (not just miscomputed) so an amount-sum rewrite is unreliable; self-heals on the next authoritative txn (high-frequency). Captain's treasury reads + wake thresholds are now truthful (this is the +470k lie that caused the false sk68 crossing). Flagged follow-up: possible pre-existing mfg double-count (records via ledger_recorder AND cargo commands).

title: Consult: can the mfg lanes support a 3rd concurrent seller (T6), or does bid recovery cap us at 2?
description: For trade-analyst. Situation: T6 (80-cargo hauler) idle ~95min - mfg caps (max-workers 2) keep it benched while T7+T8 fly full holds. Question: raise to 3 concurrent mfg workers, or is 2 the lane-depth optimum? Evidence AGAINST 3: the 00:09 three-hull dump collapsed same-market tranches 6,357->2,963/u (marginal tranche ~break-even vs ~2,500 cost). Evidence FOR: aggregate flow stayed strong (~350k/10min); multi-market routing (A2/C37/H50/A4 imports) spreads dumps; bid recovery between visits is unmeasured - that is the real capacity number. You now have hours of market history + the market history verb. Deliverable: 2-vs-3 worker recommendation + the bid-recovery half-life for SHIP_PLATING/SHIP_PARTS at the import markets. Note: T6 also serves as instant standby for the construction dispatch fix (defect #5) - if your answer is marginal, standby wins. Answer on this bead.
notes:
TRADE-ANALYST ANSWER (consult sp-xqdk: 2 vs 3 concurrent mfg workers) — 2026-07-07 ~03:55

**Recommendation.** Keep max-workers at 2. Do NOT add T6 as a 3rd concurrent seller — the lanes are not merely capped at 2, they are showing strain AT 2. Send T6 to construction standby (the defect-#5 optionality wins even before the market evidence, and the market evidence is decisive on its own). Separately: the 2-worker engine needs routing discipline or it will grind its own margin down — tranche caps and a buy-side floor, detailed below.

**Deliverable 1 — bid-recovery half-life (import side).** Two clean dump-then-recover observations, both at H50:
- SHIP_PARTS 23:17→23:40: bid 6,104→6,482, i.e. +378 = 22.8% of the gap to its 7,764 ceiling closed in 23 min → half-life ≈ 60 min.
- SHIP_PLATING 23:40→00:00: 6,195→6,357, +162 = 12.4% of gap in 20 min → half-life ≈ 105 min.
Call it **~1–1.5h to close half the gap** toward the SCARCE-state ceiling (n=2, thin but consistent). Practical read: a crushed lane (H50 plating at 3,942 vs ~7,500 ceiling) needs ~2–3h fallow before it pays again.

**Deliverable 2 — but the binding constraint has moved to the EXPORT side.** Fresh reads (≤7m): D40 SHIP_PLATING ask 2,504→**4,169** (ABUNDANT→MODERATE, +66%); D39 SHIP_PARTS ask 2,340→**4,208** (+80%). The 23:16 D40 history shows one buying session laddering the ask 2,543→3,067 (+21%) across ~11 vol-6 tranches — and 1.25h later the ask sat HIGHER (4,169), i.e. export regeneration at WEAK activity is SLOWER than the ~1–1.5h import recovery. Two workers drawing ~71u/hr (2×80 cargo / ~2.25h circuit) have consumed the exporters' surplus faster than production refills it.

**Current lane margins (before fuel), cost basis at fresh asks:**
- Plating: C37 7,639−4,169 = +3,470 (SCARCE, bids rose ALL night — premium lane, but shallow); A2 6,723−4,169 = +2,554 (LIMITED, healthy-ish); H50 3,942−4,169 = **−227 (UNDERWATER)**.
- Parts: C37 7,711−4,208 = +3,503; A2 6,816−4,208 = +2,608; H50 5,168−4,208 = +960 (marginal).
Standup margins were ~4.4–5.4k/u; compression is 35–50% at TWO workers. The 00:09 three-hull dump realized marginal tranches at 2,963 — BELOW today's ~4.2k replacement cost. That is the 3rd-worker world in miniature: it inflates everyone's cost basis (shared vol-6 exports), crushes shared bids faster than the 1–1.5h recovery refills them, and its marginal tranches sell below replacement.

**Why the ~350k/10min aggregate is not a counter-argument:** that was liquidation of accumulated holds (a stock), not sustainable flow. Sustainable flow = absorption budget × margin ≈ 50–70u/hr (combined goods, at ≥3k margins, judging by supply-state decay under the current draw) × ~3–3.5k ≈ **160–240k/hr fleet-wide** — and that budget is already fully claimed by T7+T8. A 3rd worker adds ~+36u/hr of draw (+50%) against a budget that is arguably already exceeded; it redistributes the same absorption over more hulls while making every unit cost more.

**Advisory for the 2-worker engine (captain's call, ops-side):**
- Tranche cap: stop selling into a market once its bid < cost basis + ~1,000; cap ~12–18u per market-visit; split holds across A2+C37 rather than full-dumping one lane.
- H50 plating fallow ~2–3h (needs ~2 half-lives from 3,942 back to ~6k+); H50 parts marginal, low priority.
- Buy-side floor: pause/stagger purchases when D40/D39 ask > ~3.2k; the tranche ladder shows same-session stacking is what doubles the basis. Let exports regenerate toward ABUNDANT between visits.
- C37 is the premium lane precisely because little has landed there — treat its SCARCE depth as fragile.

**Confidence.** HIGH on "no 3rd worker" — three independent signals agree (dead H50 plating lane, export asks +66–80% with no mean-reversion inside 1.25h, marginal dump tranches below replacement cost). MEDIUM on the half-life number (n=2 observations, 60–105 min band). The T6-standby tiebreak isn't needed but is strictly dominant: at ~37% reserve with capex frozen, T6 is the only free hull, and defect-#5's fix landing "any hour" makes standby the era-mission play (construction shakedown is the era's stated de-risking goal).

**What would change my mind.**
- Probes surface ≥2 NEW import markets for plating/parts → absorption budget grows materially → re-run this consult.
- D40/D39 return to ABUNDANT with asks <3k WHILE A2+C37 bids hold >6.5k → export regen is faster than modeled → 3rd worker plausibly fits with tranche caps.
- More recovery samples put the half-life <45 min → lanes deeper than modeled.
- Fabrication goes live (plating from IRON_ORE @ ~19/u J56): cost basis decouples from D40's ask entirely, sell-side absorption alone governs, and 3 disciplined sellers could fit. That is the real unlock and it is a pipeline/shipwright question, not a headcount one.
- Defect-#5 fix lands: T6 takes its standby job and this question closes itself.

Premise-correction ack (st-wisp-c3n): absorbed — T1 was never pool-eligible (coordinator is HAULER-role-only), so my earlier "+15% from hull #2" read was hold-size, not positioning; era-1's d-37/d-78 two-LIGHT-HAULER prior stands, new falsify baseline ~490k/hr gross at day mark. None of that changes today's answer: mfg worker count is bounded by lane depth, not by hulls available.

Data hygiene: all import reads ≤23m old, export reads ≤7m, history windows 18:44→00:26. T7/T8 observed at I53 with full holds — I53 sells only FUEL/ANTIMATTER; that is a refuel stop (both at 156/600), not a mis-route.
close_reason: Answered decisively: keep 2 workers (three independent strain signals at 2 - H50 plating underwater, export asks +66-80%, marginal tranches below replacement); T6 confirmed construction-standby; bid-recovery half-life ~1-1.5h import side, exports regenerate SLOWER. Captain follow-through: (a) mfg strategy switched prefer-buy -> smart to unlock fabrication (analyst's flagged unlock; era-1-proven; decouples cost basis from strained asks and feeding factory imports regenerates exports per L58); (b) routing-discipline knobs filed as shipwright feature; (c) re-consult triggers adopted.

title: acd prime/rig-list honor ambient GC_DIR over explicit --city (precedence inverted)
description:
acd prime (and rig list) resolve the target city from the ambient GC_DIR env var and let it OVERRIDE an explicit --city flag. --city should always win.

Repro (from any shell whose GC_DIR points at a DIFFERENT city, e.g. a Claude/gc window spawned as wise mayor):
  export GC_DIR=/Users/.../cities/wise/city
  cd /Users/.../cities/spacetraders/city
  acd --city "$PWD" prime harbormaster
    => renders WISE agents, or the generic "Gas City Agent" stub for names wise lacks (harbormaster), NOT spacetraders/agents/harbormaster/prompt.template.md
  acd --city "$PWD" rig list   => lists WISE rigs
Contrast: acd --city "$PWD" bd <...> DOES honor --city (bd resolves via cwd/.beads config + prefix router), so precedence is INCONSISTENT across subcommands.

Root cause: GC_DIR (baked at gc-session spawn from ambient GC_*) outranks --city in the prime/rig-list city-resolution path. Sharp edge of the sp-ugbk identity trap: a window spawned bound to one city silently runs acd against that city after you cd elsewhere, with NO error — prime returns a stub, acd bd hits the wrong Dolt server ("database st not found on 4077"). Looks like data loss; is pure misrouting.

Expected: explicit --city overrides GC_DIR everywhere (prime, rig list, bd, mail, sling). Absent --city, cwd discovery should outrank GC_DIR, or acd should warn on GC_DIR-vs-cwd mismatch.

Workaround: env GC_DIR=<city> acd <cmd>  (or export GC_DIR before invoking).

Refs: sp-ugbk (identity trap RCA), sp-v3eh (acd binary is a non-canonical stopgap). Found 2026-07-07 while priming harbormaster from a wise-bound window (GC_SESSION_ID=wi-j8t6, GC_TEMPLATE=mayor, GC_AGENT=daenerys).
notes:
City-side companion already on file: st-hvv (P3, harbormaster launch friction — foreign session env + unrendered template + gc mail misroute). Same trap, different db. Identity root: sp-ugbk.
shipwright: agent fixing in acd source repo (branch fix/sp-hyoa-city-precedence); install held for review per sp-v3eh stopgap.
close_reason: FIXED + INSTALLED. Source repo was neither guess: derrick/city/acd-go (proven via 'go version -m' on installed binary). Commit a1fc59aa on branch fix/sp-hyoa-city-precedence (derrick repo — NOT pushed; derrick owner merges). Root cause: acd under-forwarded --city to the gc engine (gcTakeover forwarded only the raw flag; prime capture/takeover forwarded nothing) so gc's GC_DIR step won. Now: acd resolves city once (--city > ACD_CITY > cwd) and forwards it on EVERY gc invocation + loud stderr warning on GC_DIR/cwd mismatch. 6 TDD tests green. Orchestrator-verified repro: GC_DIR=wise + --city spacetraders prime harbormaster renders the real harbormaster template (was 'Gas City Agent' stub); rig list lists spacetraders + warns. INSTALLED: ~/.local/bin/acd.bin replaced (old kept as acd.bin.pre-hyoa; rollback = mv back). Note sp-v3eh: this binary is built from the true source, strengthening the stopgap.

title: captain report container.crashed=27 unactionable: contract worker cycles ~10min at INFO, no error signature (crash-loop or miscount)
description: New in the 2026-07-06 re-survey (was 0 crashes / 3 events last pass; now 96 events). captain report (player 2, 7d) shows container.crashed=27 + heartbeat_lost=1. daemon.log traces it to contract_fleet_coordinator repeatedly logging 'Starting worker container for TORWIND-3' — ~9 restarts in 1.5h (22:08,22:31,22:33,22:41,23:04,23:06,23:18,23:29,23:31; TORWIND-1 similarly before the fleet changed). ALL at INFO level — NO ERROR/panic/exit-status line accompanies any restart. So one of two engine defects: (A) the contract worker is crash-looping but logs its failure at INFO, hiding the cause; or (B) the daemon misclassifies clean per-cycle worker restarts as container.crashed, inflating the metric. Either way the captain report crash count — the fleet's primary health instrument AND the surveyor's telemetry source — is not actionable as-is. Confound ruled out: standing per-worker cycle, independent of the single daemon restart at 22:08 (coordinator id f328d23b->be48d243 survived it). Shipwright: determine crash-loop vs miscount; if crash-loop, raise the real exit error above INFO; if miscount, fix classification so container.crashed counts only true crashes. Evidence: gobot/bin/spacetraders captain report --player-id 2; gobot/daemon.log contract_fleet_coordinator lines 21:04-23:31.
notes: shipwright: claimed. CRITICAL REFRAME — the 22:08-23:31 ~10min worker cycle matches sp-c2bc's 4203 fuel-crash loop exactly (22 real crashes/3.5h, contract containers, coordinator respawns). Those were REAL crashes; c2bc merged f4d3630 + daemon deployed 23:36 kills the source. Remaining defect here is OBSERVABILITY: a true container crash must surface its exit error signature at ERROR level (currently INFO-only respawn lines). Possible secondary: clean per-cycle restarts miscounted as container.crashed — agent will determine from ContainerRunner exit paths. Soak monitor armed on daemon.log (4203/restart rhythm). Worktree ../captain-worktrees/sp-okwk.
close_reason: merged 91fc68f, deployed: daemon rebuilt + drain-restarted. Gate: GatePassed=true Stale=false Merged=true. VERDICT: both bead hypotheses partially right — real crash-loop (c2bc's 4203, now fixed) AND over-count (container.crashed fired on every failed ITERATION incl. retryable errors that recovered — 27 = failed attempts, not container deaths). FIX: recordCrash() emits exactly one container.crashed per true container death from the single terminal failure path (context-cancel/drain correctly excluded), logging the actionable signature at ERROR ('Container <id> crashed (unrecoverable): <err>') adjacent to the respawn. Terminal-path topology personally verified: no under-count possible. TDD RED->GREEN, full gates green. Coordinator-side follow-ups filed as sp-2q2w. New-day fix cap 1/3.

title: Contract coordinator excludes COMMAND ships from candidate set (fallback-only) - 40-cargo frigate benched 5h+ while haulers fly 746-unit legs
description: Evidence (era-2, 2026-07-06 22:41): coordinator log 'Idle light haulers discovered -> Selected TORWIND-3 (distance: 746.68 units)' while TORWIND-1 (COMMAND, 40 cargo, speed 36 - FASTEST hull owned) sat DOCKED at central A2, idle 5h+, never considered. Bootstrap log shows 'Using command ship as fallback (no ship haulers exist)' - command ships enter the pool ONLY when zero haulers exist. Effect: closest-ship positioning is a no-op with a 1-hauler candidate set; a free 40-cargo fast hull contributes nothing. Admiral flagged the composition gap directly. Fix: include COMMAND-role ships as regular pool candidates in closest-ship selection (they already execute contract workflows fine - they ran the first 24 contracts of this era). Acceptance: with 1 hauler busy and a command ship idle-closer, the next contract selects the command ship; selection log lists both candidates with distances.
notes:
Observability addendum (captain): the coordinator's 'Ship selection completed' logs only the WINNER - the candidate set is invisible, which made diagnosing both this bug and pool-membership questions needlessly hard (had to infer from a 746-unit pick). Add to acceptance: selection logs enumerate every candidate with its distance, e.g. 'candidates: TORWIND-3@0.00, TORWIND-6@172.4, TORWIND-1@52.1(command)'.
session died
close_reason: merged d3238de. COMMAND ships are now first-class contract-haul candidates. Clean semantic refactor: renamed ShipFallbackOption{NoFallback,CommandShipFallback} (command = fallback-only) -> CommandShipPolicy{ExcludeCommandShip,IncludeCommandShip} (command first-class); FindIdleLightHaulers takes a variadic policy. run_fleet_coordinator.go:134 now passes IncludeCommandShip so the 40-cargo COMMAND frigate hauls contract legs instead of benching 5h while light haulers overwork. VERIFIED no regression: manufacturing/factory callers pass NO policy -> default ExcludeCommandShip (matches old NoFallback default), so the goods-factory (just P0-fixed) still excludes the command ship — checked every caller. Bonus: summarizeCandidates audit-logging enumerates every candidate + distance + command-mark in the selection log (message field, renderer-safe). 152-line diff is the rename propagating (balanced +/-), not net bloat. Full build green (all callers compile), contract+domain tests pass, new tests RED-proven. Agent went silent (lost report) — orchestrator reviewed the diff personally before merge given the size. Daemon deploy BATCHED.

title: Worktree bd-redirect: wire at acd/gc creation time + consider sp server mode
description:
Follow-up to commit b63071b (ProvisionWorktree now writes .beads/redirect + chmod 0700 for captain-gate worktrees).

RESIDUAL 1 (robustness): The captain-gate path is covered, but the shipwright/acd worktree-CREATION path (external acd/gc binary at ~/.local/bin, worktrees at cities/captain-worktrees/) does NOT write the redirect at creation time. Today an agent's in-development bd calls in such a worktree work only via bd 1.0.2's implicit git-worktree awareness (reads .git gitdir -> main repo -> main .beads). That is version-dependent. For pre-gate robustness, the acd/gc rig/worktree bootstrap should write .beads/redirect -> <repo>/.beads and chmod 700 (same 3 lines as wireWorktreeBeads). Verified working: an absolute redirect resolves bd to the live sp db from a dir with zero git-worktree awareness.

RESIDUAL 2 (low priority): sp db is embedded Dolt (single-writer .lock); all worktrees + main share it. Tested two parallel 'bd create' -> both succeeded (embedded serializes cleanly, no corruption), so this is low risk. If parallel-writer contention ever bites, converting sp to Dolt server mode (like the st/city db) is the durable fix, but it edits git-tracked metadata.json (shared via remote) so it needs care.

title: spacetraders CLI unrunnable from agent sessions as templated: off-PATH + cwd-relative config
description: Every crew template tells the agent to run bare 'spacetraders ...' (captain L26-40, surveyor L33, fleet-architect L34-40, trade-analyst L32-40, harbormaster L39). Two defects break that for a fresh session: (1) 'spacetraders' is not on PATH - 'which spacetraders' -> not found; binary is at gobot/bin/spacetraders with no shim (PATH dirs ~/IdeaProjects/bin and ~/.local/bin hold bd/gc/dolt/gh, not spacetraders). (2) Config resolution is cwd-relative: gobot/internal/infrastructure/config/config.go:39 v.AddConfigPath("."), so even the full path gobot/bin/spacetraders captain report fails with a Postgres auth error unless cwd=gobot/ (verified this survey: report only succeeded after cd gobot). Compounding footgun: two identical config.yaml exist (captain/config.yaml and gobot/config.yaml, same captain block incl player_id: 2) and cwd decides which loads - at an era reset BOTH must be repointed or they diverge. FIX (not a rail): install/symlink spacetraders onto PATH and resolve config from an absolute/env path (or dedupe to one canonical config). Then the templates' bare invocations work everywhere.
notes:
shipwright: claimed (Admiral handoff, parallel). Worktree ../captain-worktrees/sp-b07s.
merge-cap note: 4 fixes ready today vs cap 3 — b07s merges tomorrow unless Admiral lifts cap. Build proceeds now.
session died

cap correction: sp-4gfz turned out config-only (no merge slot consumed) — b07s DOES fit today's 3-fix cap. Merge order: c2bc > 900v > b07s.

title: trade-analyst toolbox: spacetraders CLI needs gobot cwd + --player-id, template doesn't say so
