title: friction: (s86) No pipeline TASK visibility: `construction status` prints only pipeline status/progress ("EXECUTING, 0.0
description: friction: (s86) No pipeline TASK visibility: `construction status` prints only pipeline status/progress ("EXECUTING, 0.0%") and there is no verb to list a pipeline's tasks with their states (PENDING/READY/ASSIGNED + gating reason). Diagnosing WHY the gate got zero worker-assignments for 5h required a code-read agent over the coordinator source. An `operations tasks <pipeline-id>` (or `construction status --tasks`) readout would make queue starvation observable in one command.

title: friction: (s86) No market-search-by-good verb: answering "where does good X trade in this system" (the Admiral's gas eva
description: friction: (s86) No market-search-by-good verb: answering "where does good X trade in this system" (the Admiral's gas evaluation, and locating an ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY producer) requires `market get` across all 29 cached markets one waypoint at a time. A `market find <GOOD> --system X1-PZ28` (scan the cached market table, print waypoints + prices + supply) would turn a ~30-command sweep into one read.

title: friction: (s85) `construction start` is a SILENT FOOTGUN: it creates the pipeline row (pipeline_type=CONSTRUCTION) + tas
description: friction: (s85) `construction start` is a SILENT FOOTGUN: it creates the pipeline row (pipeline_type=CONSTRUCTION) + tasks and returns "Status: PLANNING", but spawns NO coordinator container and returns NO container id — the pipeline sits INERT at 0.0% forever with nothing executing it. The executor is the MANUFACTURING coordinator, which adopts pipelines ONLY at startup recovery (`FindByStatus(PLANNING,EXECUTING)`, `run_parallel_manufacturing_coordinator.go` recoverState at ~line 200) and NEVER re-polls the table in its main loop. So a construction pipeline created AFTER the coordinator booted is never seen. The undocumented required sequence is: `construction start` FIRST, THEN bounce `operations --manufacturing` so recovery adopts it. Candidate fix: either (a) `construction start` should signal the running coordinator to re-scan (or spawn its own coordinator), or (b) the coordinator loop should periodically re-poll for new PLANNING pipelines, or at minimum (c) `construction start` should WARN "no coordinator running — start operations --manufacturing to execute this pipeline". Nothing about the success output hints the pipeline is inert.
close_reason: friction dup — resolved by sp-hkfb (construction start silent no-adoption footgun fixed). sp-tme0 sweep.

title: friction: (s84) Measuring manufacturing standalone $/h still requires manual ledger archaeology: `--type SELL_CARGO` cle
description: friction: (s84) Measuring manufacturing standalone $/h still requires manual ledger archaeology: `--type SELL_CARGO` cleanly isolates trading REVENUE (L56) but the BUY side (PURCHASE_CARGO) is shared with contract cargo sourcing, so per-stream NET is hand-paired by timestamp — and the ledger prints in UTC-3 while container logs are UTC, forcing a 3h offset correction to align a sell to its task. A `ledger list --category TRADING_REVENUE --by-ship` or a per-container P&L rollup (revenue − costs − fuel, tagged by the worker container that generated it) would make stream-level $/h a one-command read instead of a cross-timezone reconciliation.

title: friction: (s84) `construction status` shows the material BILL (FAB_MATS/ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY/QUANTUM_STABILIZERS remaining
description: friction: (s84) `construction status` shows the material BILL (FAB_MATS/ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY/QUANTUM_STABILIZERS remaining) but NO cost estimate and there is no `construction start --dry-run` — so I cannot pre-price the gate-build material spend (nor its hauler-cycle count) before `construction start` commits haulers and capital. Every other capital commit (shipyard purchase --budget) has a ceiling knob; the gate build has none. Candidate fix: a `construction start --dry-run` that reports estimated material cost + task count, and/or a `--budget` cap, so the biggest single mission spend isn't launched blind.

title: friction: (s81) No visibility into the contract coordinator's hauler-eligibility decision — I could not see WHY it ran o
description: friction: (s81) No visibility into the contract coordinator's hauler-eligibility decision — I could not see WHY it ran only on TORWIND-4 while TORWIND-3 (a HAULER) sat idle. Had to INFER that TORWIND-3's phantom 44/80 cargo cache benched it, confirm via a failed `ship sell` (server cargo=0), then `ship refresh`. A `coordinator status` / `contract eligible-ships` readout (which ships are in the pool, which are excluded and why: foreign cargo / assignment / distance) would turn a multi-step inference into one command — and this exact phantom-benched-hauler case is a silent $/h leak that emits no event.

title: friction: (s83) `operations status` and `operations stop --manufacturing` do NOT track a NORMALLY-launched manufacturing
description: friction: (s83) `operations status` and `operations stop --manufacturing` do NOT track a NORMALLY-launched manufacturing coordinator (no restart involved) — generalizes the s75 restart-storm item. Right after `operations start --manufacturing` succeeded and the coordinator + worker were RUNNING, `operations status` printed "No active manufacturing operations" and listed the coordinator under "Other Containers", and `operations stop --manufacturing` returned "No matching operations to stop". So the d-65 GUARD (stop the engine if it grabs the contract hauler) is non-functional as written — the only working stop is `container stop <coordinator-id>`. Candidate fix: `operations status`/`operations stop` should classify containers by type from the container DB (name prefix `parallel_manufacturing-` / recorded operation kind), not from in-memory session tracking, so a manufacturing op is stoppable/observable via its own verb whether or not a restart intervened.
close_reason: friction dup — resolved by sp-dpsq (operations status/stop made mfg-aware). era-1 friction migration, verified landed 2026-07-08 (sp-tme0 sweep).

title: friction: (s77) The startup AutoMigrate list (`../gobot internal/infrastructure/database/connection.go:86-100`) is a han
description: friction: (s77) The startup AutoMigrate list (`../gobot internal/infrastructure/database/connection.go:86-100`) is a hand-maintained enumeration of only 11 models and silently EXCLUDES the entire manufacturing subsystem (ManufacturingPipeline/Task/FactoryState/Dependency) plus gas/storage models. So every new persisted field on those models is a latent `column ... does not exist (SQLSTATE 42703)` that only fires on the first live persist — this is the THIRD such missing-migration (s73 ships.reserved, s77 manufacturing_pipelines.sequence_number, and the ce10b92 fix only patched the 11 listed models). Candidate: register ALL persistence models in AutoMigrate (or add a CI schema-drift check diffing GORM struct tags against the applied schema) so this class stops recurring one column at a time.
close_reason: friction dup — resolved by sp-tvcm (AutoMigrate/schema-drift gate); column-level follow-up tracked separately in sp-s0mw. sp-tme0 sweep.

title: friction: (s77) RESOLUTION of the s76 item above — the "untracked report is invisible to the pipeline" fear was a FALSE
description: friction: (s77) RESOLUTION of the s76 item above — the "untracked report is invisible to the pipeline" fear was a FALSE ALARM. The d-83 report merged (`status: merged`, fix commit 3e5dc4f on main) THIS session despite still showing `??` untracked in the local working tree. So the fix pipeline DOES reach a report that the local `git status` calls untracked (it works from its own worktree/source, not the Captain's uncommitted local copy). The s76 candidate fixes are therefore lower priority than I flagged — untracked-locally ≠ stranded. Keep the "surface uncommitted status:new report" idea only as a nice-to-have observability aid, not a blocker fix.
close_reason: resolution-note bead, no action — documents that the s76 'untracked report invisible to pipeline' fear was a FALSE alarm. Closing the record (sp-tme0 sweep).

title: friction: (s76) A FILED fix report can sit UNTRACKED in git and never reach the fix pipeline. The d-83 report (mission's
description: friction: (s76) A FILED fix report can sit UNTRACKED in git and never reach the fix pipeline. The d-83 report (mission's sole blocker) is `status:new` but shows `??` in `git status` — the fix pipeline builds from git worktrees (committed files only), so an uncommitted report is invisible to it. All of s75's `state/*` output is likewise uncommitted, so it may just await the normal state-committer, BUT the Captain has NO git access (CLI-only actuator) and cannot commit it, and the situation-report gives no signal that a `status:new` report is uncommitted. Candidate fixes: (a) report-filing auto-stages/commits the report file; (b) the situation-report surfaces "uncommitted status:new report exists"; (c) confirm the captain-state committer stages `reports/` and not just `state/`. Without one of these a filed blocker can silently cost sessions.
close_reason: resolved (false alarm) — the concern (filed fix report sits untracked, never reaches pipeline) was investigated and found false per sp-1fc3. sp-tme0 sweep.

title: friction: (s75) `operations stop --manufacturing` FAILED to stop a restart-looping manufacturing coordinator ("no matchi
description: friction: (s75) `operations stop --manufacturing` FAILED to stop a restart-looping manufacturing coordinator ("no matching operations") because it lost its operation tracking across the daemon restarts; only `container stop <container-id>` worked (it persists STATUS=STOPPED to the DB, which recovery honors). Candidate fix: `operations stop` should reconcile target operations from the container DB (the same source recovery uses), not from in-memory/session tracking that a restart wipes — otherwise the one command meant to halt a runaway operation is exactly the one that fails during a restart storm.
close_reason: friction dup — resolved by sp-dpsq (operations status/stop made mfg-aware). era-1 friction migration, verified landed 2026-07-08 (sp-tme0 sweep).

title: friction: (s75) The contract coordinator's select-closest bought PRECIOUS_STONES FRESH on empty TORWIND-4 (distance 714)
description: friction: (s75) The contract coordinator's select-closest bought PRECIOUS_STONES FRESH on empty TORWIND-4 (distance 714) while TORWIND-3 sat idle already holding 65 units of exactly that good (the contract's required good) — selection is position-only and ignores existing matching cargo, so it re-buys goods a hauler already carries. Candidate fix: bias ship selection toward a hauler already carrying the required contract good (or at least skip the re-buy leg when a held hauler can fulfill), cutting a full buy-leg's travel + cargo spend.

title: friction: (s75) THE FIX-PIPELINE GATE (build+tests) IS BLIND TO A COORDINATOR THAT CRASHES THE WHOLE DAEMON ON LAUNCH —
description: friction: (s75) THE FIX-PIPELINE GATE (build+tests) IS BLIND TO A COORDINATOR THAT CRASHES THE WHOLE DAEMON ON LAUNCH — same class as the s73 missing-migration blind spot. `operations start --manufacturing` panics the daemon in a restart loop because the parallel-manufacturing handler's `eventSubscriber`/`eventPublisher` are never wired in main.go (they're nil), and the command runs on a naked goroutine with no recover() (L53, report d-83). Tests pass; a live launch takes down the process. CANDIDATE FIXES: (a) a gate smoke-test that actually launches EACH coordinator type against a throwaway daemon and asserts it survives ≥1 iteration past state-recovery; (b) a top-level `recover()` in ContainerRunner.execute() so no single coordinator panic can ever kill the whole daemon (containment — turns a process-death into a container FAILED).

title: friction: (s73) THE FIX-PIPELINE GATE CANNOT CATCH A MISSING-MIGRATION REGRESSION. The reservation feature (d-79) merged
description: friction: (s73) THE FIX-PIPELINE GATE CANNOT CATCH A MISSING-MIGRATION REGRESSION. The reservation feature (d-79) merged with green tests yet took the WHOLE contract earner down in production: it added GORM struct tags (Reserved/ReservationReason on ShipModel) but no SQL migration, and the test DB AutoMigrates tags while production requires hand-applied migrations/*.sql that were never authored (SQLSTATE 42703 on every ship Save; L52). The build+test gate is structurally blind to this class. CANDIDATE FIX (high value — this outage cost hours of zero income): add a schema-drift check to the gate that compares GORM model tags against the applied `migrations/` DDL (or runs the production migration path in CI) and FAILS a schema-touching feature that lacks its migration. Second-order gap: the Captain has no way to apply a DB migration (CLI-only actuator, no psql) — a migration fix requires an out-of-band operator step even after code merges, so schema fixes have a longer restore-latency than pure-code fixes; a `spacetraders admin migrate` verb (or auto-run migrations on daemon boot) would let the daemon self-repair schema drift on restart.
close_reason: friction dup — resolved by sp-tvcm (AutoMigrate/schema-drift gate); column-level follow-up tracked separately in sp-s0mw. sp-tme0 sweep.

description: friction: (s71) NOTE for meta-review: the ship-reservation/exclusion flag (top tooling ask above, s58/s67) is now FILED as a kind:feature report (reports/bugs/2026-07-03-ship-reservation-flag.md, d-79) on Admiral order — track it through the pipeline rather than re-queuing it here.

title: friction: (s71) `goods produce <good>` has NO `--dry-run`, and `operations start --manufacturing --dry-run` echoes only
description: friction: (s71) `goods produce <good>` has NO `--dry-run`, and `operations start --manufacturing --dry-run` echoes only the coordinator SETTINGS (prefer-fabricate / min-price / workers / pipelines) — neither previews the acquisition plan (which ship it would claim, the buy-vs-fabricate tree for a specific good, or estimated cost). So the Admiral-ordered "study the fabrication economics before committing" is only partially answerable read-only; the real per-good plan requires a LIVE run + `container logs`, which for a hauler-hungry engine is not a safe dry study on a one-hauler fleet. Candidate fix: a `--dry-run`/`--plan` on `goods produce` (and a richer manufacturing dry-run) that prints the resolved dependency tree, buy-vs-fabricate decisions, ship count needed, and estimated credits — so the economics can be evaluated before actuation.

title: friction: (s67) The SAME missing ship-exclusion/reservation flag applies to `construction start` — it too auto-claims id
description: friction: (s67) The SAME missing ship-exclusion/reservation flag applies to `construction start` — it too auto-claims idle haulers with no `--ships`/`--exclude-ships`. Now that the discovery verb shipped and I've read the jump-gate bill (X1-PZ28-I67: FAB_MATS 1600 + ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY 400, fabricate-only), BOTH mission threads (gate-build + manufacturing) converge on the same need: a hauler pinned to the campaign without racing the contract earner (L46c). This elevates the reservation flag from a manufacturing nicety to the single binding constraint on BOTH mission horizons — a fleet-level ship-reservation (or `--exclude-ships`) shared by manufacturing + construction is now the top tooling ask. GOOD (resolved, note for meta-review): the waypoint/system-discovery verb ask is DONE — `waypoint list/get` shipped and works end-to-end; the jump-gate is now locatable and its construction status readable.

title: friction: (s58) `operations start --manufacturing` (and `goods produce`) exposes NO ship-exclusion/reservation flag — it
description: friction: (s58) `operations start --manufacturing` (and `goods produce`) exposes NO ship-exclusion/reservation flag — it auto-discovers idle haulers, unlike gas extraction which takes explicit `--siphons`/`--storage`. With a single productive hauler that is also the contract earner, this makes a clean *parallel* manufacturing experiment impossible: the engine will race the contract coordinator (L46c) for the same ship, and I can't fence it off. Candidate fix: a `--ships`/`--exclude-ships` flag (or a fleet-level ship-reservation) for manufacturing so a stream can be pinned to a dedicated hauler without stealing the contract earner. This is the actual gate on validating manufacturing $/h without corrupting the contract loop.

title: friction: (s55/s63/s70) The L28 desynced-Balance false alarm RECURRED a 5th time (s39/s51/s55/s63/s70): a corrupt PURCHA
description: friction: (s55/s63/s70) The L28 desynced-Balance false alarm RECURRED a 5th time (s39/s51/s55/s63/s70): a corrupt PURCHASE_CARGO Balance row (-18,197 s55; -2,660 s63; -69,489 s70) fires FOUR spurious credits.threshold DOWN events (100k/250k/500k/1M) + a negative 24h delta in the fleet report, forcing a full hand re-derivation of true treasury before any action. Now a chronic, per-few-sessions tax. Candidate fix (queued since s51): reconcile the ledger Balance column, or compute credits.threshold off the last CONTRACT_* anchor instead of the raw row, so a single desynced row can't manufacture a fleet-wide treasury panic.

If wanted: survey the surviving 21 memories, identify additional stable doctrine to promote into the template, get Admiral sign-off, promote + delete (backup first, sp-4ek3 pattern).

title: Beads pre-commit hook exports+stages issues.jsonl (worktree-root misfire) — export-without-staging or worktree-aware
description: sp-ezar follow-up (out of Go-module scope). core.hooksPath=.beads/hooks runs 'bd hooks run pre-commit' which exports the beads DB to issues.jsonl and STAGES it on every git commit in the shared checkout; in a worktree (no .beads/ layout) it misfires the export to the worktree ROOT (./issues.jsonl). This is beads TOOLING, not this repo's Go module — needs the hook made export-WITHOUT-staging or worktree-path-aware. LOW priority: sp-ezar's commit-tree gate bypasses the hook entirely so gated merges are already immune; this is only about stray root exports + code commits carrying issues.jsonl when made via plain 'git commit' (mitigate with --no-verify meanwhile, which shipwright already does).

title: Optional: restore mfg task-linkage attribution on the (now single) cargo ledger row
description: Flagged during sp-uytq double-count fix. The removed ManufacturingLedgerRecorder rows carried richer attribution (task_id, factory, supply_level, net_profit, OperationType='manufacturing', entity='manufacturing_task'). The surviving authoritative cargo-command ledger row (sc6u) attributes via the ctx OperationContext (container/op-type when a mfg OperationContext is on ctx, else 'manual'). Treasury AMOUNT is now correct (counted once) — this is NOT a regression, since no query/metric consumes the old mfg metadata (P&L groups by category+amount; per-task data lives in the manufacturing_tasks table). This bead only matters IF we later want per-mfg-task linkage visible ON the ledger row itself: thread a mfg OperationContext (task_id/factory) onto ctx at the PurchaseCargo/SellCargo call sites in purchaser.go/seller.go so the single authoritative row inherits it. Low value; do not action unless ledger-level mfg attribution is explicitly wanted.

Cleanup: delete the registry line + any dangling ContainerType/enum refs; verify build + the command-factory pin test still green. Low-pri: it can't be spawned so it can't misbehave; deleting just ends the confusion.

title: shipyard purchase --waypoint false-fails on empty cache instead of refreshing
description: Admiral-approved 2026-07-07. L49: 'shipyard purchase' with a pinned --waypoint FALSE-FAILS against an empty shipyard cache (reads stale/empty cache and reports failure instead of refreshing); the workaround folklore is 're-buy WITHOUT --waypoint so auto-discover visits and reads fresh'. FIX: on empty/stale cache for an explicitly pinned waypoint, refresh (visit/read) before failing — an explicit waypoint should be MORE reliable than auto-discovery, not less. RETIRES L49's re-buy clause on landing.
close_reason: merged 05df3e3. Real defect (agent correctly ignored my brief's case/trait speculation, diagnosed from bead): 'shipyard purchase --waypoint' false-failed on a FRESH shipyard. SpaceTraders /shipyard omits the priced 'ships' array until one of your ships is present at the waypoint (returns only 'shipTypes'); the batch handler read listings UP FRONT for budget math BEFORE the purchase loop navigates there -> FindListingByType miss -> hard abort 'ship type not available'. FIX: getShipPriceFromShipyard returns listingReady bool, discriminated via always-present shipTypes — HasShipType+empty-priced = empty cache -> DEFER (purchasableCount=Quantity, keep pinned waypoint so the loop visits+reads fresh, bounded by per-purchase credit guard); type absent from shipTypes = genuine error, fail fast. Symmetric with auto-discover, which dodged this by skipping the pre-read (the 're-buy WITHOUT --waypoint' folklore, L49). 3 TDD tests incl. over-defer guard, RED-proven, gates green. Daemon deploy BATCHED with sp-hs2j restart. Retires L49 re-buy clause — flag sp-4ek3.

title: batch-contract --iterations is a dead flag: runs one contract regardless of N
description: Admiral-approved 2026-07-07. L43: 'workflow batch-contract --iterations N' runs exactly ONE contract regardless of N — the flag is dead. Honor it or remove it (removal + doc pointing at 'contract start' for continuous operation is acceptable; a flag that silently does nothing corrupts operator mental models and cost a session of confusion in era 1). RETIRES L43's first clause on landing.
close_reason: merged cbad21e. Removed the dead batch-contract --iterations flag end-to-end (Go layer: CLI flag/validation/echo, client param + req/resp fields, ignored daemon param + misleading TODO, gRPC pass-through). Evidence it was dead: container_ops_contract.go delegated to ContractWorkflow which hardcodes iterations:=1, so any N ran exactly ONE contract (matches L43). Help now points at 'contract start' for continuous operation (which already does it via the coordinator). Proto fields left in place deliberately (P4: regen/reservation disproportionate; proto3 default 0 was already ignored — zero wire/behavior change). scout-markets --iterations untouched (separate, genuinely wired). TDD RED-proven (flag-absent test), gates green. Behavior-identical removal -> daemon deploy BATCHED with sp-hs2j's restart; CLI picks up next build-cli. Retires L43 first clause — flag sp-4ek3.

title: Generalize event burst-grouping at emission (L23) — one incident, one event, not one per retry
description: Admiral-approved 2026-07-07. L23: the event feed emits MULTIPLE rows per failure (one retry burst) and consumers must hand-group by container_id+ts before treating rows as incidents. sp-okwk fixed this for container.crashed (one event per true death). GENERALIZE: burst-group at EMISSION for the remaining multi-row event types (workflow.failed retry bursts, transient API-error bursts) — group by container_id + time window, emit one event carrying a count. Keep raw rows in logs; the EVENT stream is the captain's attention budget. RETIRES L23 on landing.
close_reason: merged c5a655f. NOT a no9i dup (verified): no9i.detectCrashLoops is CONSUME-side, counts container.crashed rows into a crashloop interrupt; kb61 is EMISSION-side, suppresses repeated workflow.failed for the same (type,ship) within 15m via the shared HasSince cooldown (BurstGroupingRecorder decorator wired at the daemon root over captainEventRepo, burst set {workflow.failed}). container.crashed DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED (grouping it would starve detectCrashLoops — locked by TestBurstGroupingLeavesContainerCrashedUngrouped). Closes a real session-burn hole: every death emits BOTH container.crashed AND interrupt-class workflow.failed; no9i demoted crashed but left workflow.failed, so a crash loop still burned a session/death via workflow.failed — now one incident = one wake. FAIL-OPEN on cooldown-check error (one-shot emission must never silently drop). Keyed on ship not container_id (respawn assigns new container_id/reuses ship — ship-keying collapses the respawn storm container_id would miss). 5 TDD tests RED-proven, gates green. Daemon deploy BATCHED with sp-hs2j restart. Retires L23 (multi-row-per-failure grouping) — flag sp-4ek3/surveyor. Follow-ups noted (not filed to avoid parallel-bd conflict): config-tunable window (mirrors sp-d3xi), optional in-payload count (needs update-or-insert store method), api.error burst when that event type lands.

title: shipwright template still documents the removed merge caps
description: Admiral removed the daily merge caps 2026-07-07 (config.yaml max_fixes/features_per_day -> 1000000, no-cap order recorded). city/agents/shipwright/prompt.template.md 'Rate limits' section still states 'at most 3 fixes and 2 features merged per day'. Template = Tier-3 rail; amend the section to reflect the no-cap ruling (gate + Admiral visibility are the quality rails) next time templates are opened with sign-off.
close_reason: friction dup — resolved by sp-4xs1 (template caps text). sp-tme0 sweep.

title: Noir-restyle mining laser beams (still pure #ff0000)
description: verify-s2 finding (2026-07-07): MiningLaserLayer.tsx:93 still draws beams in raw #ff0000, strokeWidth 0.08 — outside slice 2's declared scope but a noir-consistency gap (also a hex literal outside theme/noir.ts). Fold into slice 4 (Operational Pulse scene FX) or do standalone: NOIR.bad or a dedicated beam token via noirAlpha. Related judgment calls parked with it: engine glow radius=size*0.3 and rim shadowBlur=5 read subtle at 1x zoom (by design per review; Admiral may want them louder — same art-direction pass as st-4q3).

title: gc spams 'city pack packs/core not found' ~10x per invocation from spacetraders/city
description: Every gc command run from /Users/andres.dandrea/IdeaProjects/cities/spacetraders/city prints 'city pack "packs/core": not found, skipping: loading pack.toml: open .../city/packs/core/pack.toml: no such file or directory' 10-20 times to stderr (observed 2026-07-07, e.g. gc mail send). Harmless but drowns real output and burns agent tokens on every mail/nudge. Fix: warn ONCE per invocation (or only at -v), or stop declaring a core pack the city doesn't ship.

title: Surveyor dual-cadence 'or every N captain sessions' is unimplemented
description: surveyor/prompt.template.md:26-27 states the wake cadence is meta_review_days OR every N captain sessions, whichever comes first. Only the wall-clock branch exists: surveyorNudgeDue compares elapsed time to meta_review_days*24h (gobot/internal/captain/surveyor_nudge.go:40-45); no session-count trigger exists anywhere in the supervisor. So the 'whichever comes first' guarantee is false - a burst of many captain sessions inside one cadence window never pulls the survey forward. Low stakes today because sp-4gfz (meta_review_days unset) means neither branch fires yet; this becomes real once the cadence is switched on. FIX touches either the watchkeeper (add session-count trigger in surveyor_nudge.go) or the surveyor TEMPLATE (drop the unimplemented clause) - TIER-3 rail, human sign-off required.
notes:
TIER-3 APPROVAL: Admiral approved in-session 2026-07-07 by naming the bead directly. Batch template-hygiene worktree ../captain-worktrees/templates-batch (one agent, per-bead commits, single gate merge). sp-1bl5 shipwright ruling: drop the unimplemented dual-cadence clause from the template (P4 + cost calibration); session-count trigger can be refiled as a feature if wanted.
Admiral lifted the Tier-3 freeze 2026-07-07 and delegated to the shipwright. 'human' label removed — human sign-off is GIVEN; bead is now active shipwright work. Fix still edits templates/watchkeeper, but the safety-rail approval the engine cannot self-grant has been granted by the Admiral.
close_reason: merged d38d6bf: unimplemented 'or every N captain sessions' clause dropped from surveyor template — cadence statement now truthful (meta_review_days only). If the session-count trigger is ever wanted, refile as a feature.

title: CLI: manual cargo purchase verb missing - 'ship buy' (or market buy) blocks captain-ordered trade circuits
description: Need: buy cargo with a docked ship outside the workflow engines. Blocked TODAY (2026-07-07, sp-zc3j): trade-analyst's zero-capex mfg shakedown (TORWIND-1 circuit D40 plating + D39 parts -> A2/C37/H50 imports, ~58k working capital) cannot execute - ship has sell/dock/navigate/refuel but NO buy; market has only read verbs. Same gap will block manual gate-material purchases (FAB_MATS/ADV_CIRCUITRY direct buys) during the construction shakedown. Acceptance: 'spacetraders ship buy --ship <sym> --good <G> --units <n>' buys from the market at the ship's docked waypoint; validates cargo capacity + market availability; records PURCHASE_CARGO ledger transaction with balance; errors clearly when not docked / good not traded / insufficient credits. Mirror of existing ship sell.
notes:
Deprioritized P2->P4 by captain (2026-07-07 ~02:00Z) after Admiral challenge on need. Honest re-read: the wedge this unblocks decays in value once the flip gate opens (~1.5h); slippage is partially derivable from market history; manual trade circuits scale poorly with a wake-based captain; and the manual-fallback use case (gate materials) only matters at construction-shakedown time. KEEP the bead - the verb is legitimately useful and tiny - but it must not compete for shipwright bandwidth with sp-c2bc (live ~6-crash/hr tax on the earner, replicates per hull) or sp-900v (treasury observability). Shipwright order: sp-c2bc > sp-900v > this.
shipwright: claimed for parallel build (Admiral order: no sequencing). Worktree ../captain-worktrees/sp-71bj, branch shipwright/sp-71bj, dedicated ultracode workflow.
session died
gate JSON: {"GatePassed":true,"Stale":false,"Merged":true}. Branch commit 1227ea8 -> squash 7dfd940. 3 files, +464: cli/ship.go verb (mirror of sell, reuses existing PurchaseCargo application command), cli/ship_buy_test.go, purchase_cargo_test.go (+264 lines on the reused command). Agent-committed issues.jsonl contamination stripped pre-gate (vector noted on sp-ezar). Feature cap: 1 of 2 today.
close_reason: merged 7dfd940, deployed: bin/spacetraders rebuilt, 'ship buy' verified live (--help + validations present). Gate: GatePassed=true Stale=false Merged=true (full go build+test green).

title: history CLI: --era rejects era NAMES (runbook says --era torwind; tool wants numeric era_id)
description: Doc/impl drift found 2026-07-06 during st-wm7 Phase 5: docs/runbooks/universe-reset.md Phase 5 says 'spacetraders history summary --era <era>' with <era>=lowercase agent symbol, but cmd flag parses strconv.Atoi and errors on 'torwind'. Workaround: --era 1 (numeric) or no flag (defaults to latest closed era). Fix either side: accept names by resolving eras.name, or update the runbook (and any other history verb docs) to era_id.

title: friction: (s80) The Bash allowlist denies raw `grep`/`sed`/`find` and any `cd ../gobot && grep` compound, despite CLAUDE
description: friction: (s80) The Bash allowlist denies raw `grep`/`sed`/`find` and any `cd ../gobot && grep` compound, despite CLAUDE.md granting "full read access to the entire codebase." Read-only codebase searches must go through the `Read` tool (which needs an EXACT path — useless for discovery) or a full Explore-agent round-trip. I burned an agent dispatch on what should have been a one-line `grep -rl ManufacturingPipelineModel`. Candidate fix: allowlist a read-only search verb (e.g. `grep -r`/`rg` scoped to the repo, or restore the Grep tool) so capability/code investigation — an explicit standing obligation — doesn't cost an agent hop each time.

value: bd quirk: 'bd init --prefix X' sets only the dolt database name; the ISSUE prefix is db config key issue_prefix (underscore), which init leaves at its default - set it explicitly with 'bd config set issue_prefix X' before creating any bead, and verify with a probe create

Captain doctrine (sp-ess3): a job crash-looping on a KNOWN defect with a fix already in flight is resumed on deploy.completed, not re-rolled each heartbeat. Rationale: the captain hand-relaunched a crash-looping feeder SEVEN times against a defect whose fix was already merging, because there was no deploy signal to key on — every heartbeat looked identical to the captain, so it re-rolled the dice instead of waiting for the fix to actually land. Correct behavior: when a crash-loop event (container.crashloop) is open against a bead/component, and that bead's fix has merged, the captain should WAIT for a deploy.completed event for that build before relaunching, rather than relaunching on cadence. STATUS (2026-07-08): Part 1 (the emitter) is now IMPLEMENTED and landed on shipwright/sp-ess3 (commit d1fa53f): watchkeeper.RecordDeployIfChanged runs once at spacetraders-daemon boot (cmd/spacetraders-daemon/main.go), reads the most recent deploy.completed captain_event for the player as its baseline (no new table — internal/domain/captain EventStore.LatestByType), and emits a fresh deploy.completed event (deferred class, rides the next wake) only when the running build's commit differs from that baseline's recorded commit; an ordinary crash-restart of the same binary is a no-op. Payload carries commit/version/build_time from buildinfo.Get() plus a best-effort bead-id parsed from git log -1 --format=%s (empty if unavailable — never blocks boot). This doctrine is now actionable: watch for deploy.completed rather than assuming one exists implicitly.

L25 [d-3,d-4,d-7] — Launch heavy workflows ONE AT A TIME (absorbs L22): CONCURRENT launches hung the socket (launching-while-another-RUNS is fine). Launch → confirm health + RUNNING → next; never two at once.

STANDING DIRECTIVE (2026-07-06, Admiral, emphatic, while going AFK): NEVER depend on the Admiral or block on their input. This is an autonomous fleet/city system that MUST run fully unattended. Do NOT queue decisions, design rulings, gate approvals, memory gates, reload/deploy choices, or 'which option?' questions to the Admiral and wait — that is a design failure. Make the best call yourself from strategy + judgment, ACT, and surface results for ASYNC review only (bd notes/mail), never as a blocking question. Route all agent escalations to harbormaster, never to human. Only involve the human for genuine catastrophe or truly irreversible external action, and even then act first to keep the fleet safe. Supersedes any prior pattern of asking the Admiral to sign off.

Admiral standing ruling (2026-07-09): contracts are NEVER skipped, rejected, or filtered by value — accept and fulfill every one as fast as possible. The contract lever is TIME-TO-CLEAR (hull right-sizing, instant chaining, pre-positioning), never selection. Micro-contracts gate the next negotiation under the 1-active API cap: clearing them fast IS the value strategy.

Mail hygiene: 'gc/acd mail read <id>' does NOT reliably persist read-state through the rtk proxy — messages keep re-notifying ('[mail] You have mail...'). Use 'acd mail mark-read <id>' (or archive) to actually clear. Also: do NOT leave mail unread expecting a 'successor' session to read it — rollover/handoff was removed (sp-s8qq), captain is sole long-lived controller, so self-addressed handoff mail must be mark-read'd by you or it loops forever. Verify with 'acd mail count' (should reach 0 unread).

Friction beads MUST carry a queue label (e.g. shipwright) at creation, else they are write-only dead inventory: an unlabeled bead appears in NO polled queue (bd ready -l <role>) and ages silently while its content rots. Root of the sp-tme0 sweep — 36 era-1 'friction: (sNN)' beads accrued with zero shipwright label, invisible for weeks; ≥12 were already-fixed dups nobody noticed. RULE: when filing any friction/engine bead, label it into a real queue with an honest priority in the same breath. Ledger honesty: a backlog nobody can see is not a backlog. (Owns: harbormaster/whoever files friction; fold into the harbormaster template on the next template pass — deferred to avoid colliding with the in-flight sp-h0qs/drmf/0zx9 template edits.)

Margin and per-line P&L claims must come ONLY from a COGS-attributed source — ledger report profit-loss, or an analyst per-lane split — NEVER by subtracting a total-cost pool from a partial-revenue line. SpaceTraders books keep ONE TRADING_COSTS pool feeding BOTH market-sales COGS AND contract-delivery COGS, so computing trading margin as trade-revenue minus all-goods-cost is invalid: it double-counts contract COGS and can bury one-time mission capex (e.g. the FAB_MATS gate-construction bill) as a fake trading loss. That exact error produced a bogus trading-is-a-negative-line reframe on consult sp-f14n; the real split was TRADING +4.29M and CONTRACTS +9.47M, both positive. If attribution is not in hand, say unmeasurable — do not invent a split. Same failure class as the earlier false Postgres-down leg: narrative outran evidence.

HARD REQUIREMENT (Admiral 2026-07-09): daemon restarts MUST be resilient, because we patch the engine in REAL-TIME — every deploy = a daemon restart while the live fleet runs, so any state lost on restart means each patch damages the fleet. All operational state must survive/auto-recover across a restart. Current posture: SURVIVES — coordinators reconcile clean; dedicated-fleet/ownership is DB-persisted (l7h2 makes restart-survival a hard design requirement, reading fleet from DB not a stale startup flag); daemon is single-writer with claim-release-on-death. GAPS — (1) sp-perx factory auto-resume: DEPLOYED but NOT yet live-verified (captain flagged pending next restart); (2) sp-tgp5 OPEN: restart orphans in-flight contract-worker containers; (3) sp-w870 OPEN: ship reservations lost on restart. RULES: every daemon-changing deploy VERIFIES state-survival as part of captain acceptance; the restart-reconciliation family (sp-perx/sp-tgp5/sp-w870) is prioritized because it gates safe real-time patching; any NEW coordinator/state must persist + auto-recover on restart BY DESIGN (not a follow-up).

When a live Admiral order references an artifact ambiguously ('read your mail') and the obvious candidate is already in hand, answer with what you have and confirm the referent in ONE line — never launch a silent exhaustive search of every store first. Corollary: when executing a handoff/mail, NAME it in chat ('executing st-XXXX') so the Admiral can see the order→action link; invisible compliance reads as non-compliance.

Admiral design principle (2026-07-09, corrected me twice toward it): when protecting capacity for an operation, prefer STATIC DEDICATED ASSIGNMENT (these ships belong to X, period) over dynamic claim arbitration (priorities, tethers, guards). Simplicity beats cleverness in fleet allocation; accepted idle on dedicated hulls is a price, not a defect, when the protected line's latency matters more.

Deploy discipline (Admiral directives 2026-07-09: 'stop causing bugs' + 'ALWAYS notify captain when a fix/feature is live'). ROOT CAUSE of bug-churn: daemon regressions (sp-n0x7 unregistered handler + wrong JSON key; sp-pafv retry-budget aborting long transits; sp-snmb value-floor violating a ruling) ALL passed the gate (compile + unit tests vs FAKES) and only broke in the captain's LIVE acceptance — the production fleet was the test env. STANDING PER-BEAD PROTOCOL: (1) build in PARALLEL but DEPLOY ONE bead at a time (never batch — batch = no attribution when the fleet regresses); (2) health-check after restart (no panic, engines live) BEFORE declaring success; (3) ALWAYS notify the captain the MOMENT it is live — gc session nudge + detail in the BEAD NOTE (crew->captain mail BODIES arrive empty, so the subject/nudge is only a pointer) + the acceptance SIGNAL to watch + the REVERT path (git revert <sha>); NEVER batch deploy notes; (4) then close, or hold for captain acceptance on high-blast Tier-3. Grow the gate to catch real-API-contract mismatches pre-deploy: payload-vs-OpenAPI, handler-registration completeness, real error-shape tests (bead sp-423c). Standing Admiral rulings live in a checked-in RULINGS file every shipwright brief loads (sp-nvpn). Every brief gates on real-API/at-scale behavior, not unit-green. High-blast Tier-3 (watchkeeper/templates) deploys ALONE with captain acceptance.

Precaution: when holding a MERGED bead OPEN for a bundled/deferred deploy, add a 'MERGED <sha> - deploy-pending, do NOT rebuild' bd NOTE on it. SendMessage completion reports reach only the requesting orchestrator, not the bead, so an open merged bead with no on-bead completion record could be re-discovered as available work by another 'bd ready' coordinator. (Forward-looking discipline only — the sp-i1ku 2026-07-08 'a second agent re-built it' incident was RETRACTED as a self-messaging artifact, NOT a real duplicate agent; keep the note-on-hold habit regardless.)

Notify the captain on each DELIVERY. TWO KNOWN CONSTRAINTS (2026-07-09): (1) EMPTY-BODY BUG (fz9x): crew mail BODIES arrive empty to the captain (subject-only) — the body detail is LOST. So put the usage/capability DETAIL in a BEAD NOTE (durable; the captain reads notes), and use 'gc mail send --notify' only as a POINTER with an INFORMATIVE SUBJECT ('LIVE: ship jump + <verb>') that names the beads. Never rely on the mail body reaching them. (2) DUAL-INBOX: captain mail routes to EITHER 'shipwright' OR 'harbormaster' — sweep BOTH ('gc mail inbox shipwright' AND 'gc mail inbox') every check, or you miss half of it (I missed a batch this way tonight). Deploy = daemon restart (+ install-cli if CLI/proto changed); close beads; note the manifest; --notify pointer.

Verification doctrine (spine = L62; consolidates L20/L39/L42/L52/L60/L62). Merged is a CLAIM, not a result: declare a capability unblocked ONLY on its first observable OUTPUT — material/credits/rows — never on a merged fix. Exercise IMMEDIATELY after every merge, and re-verify capability state EVERY session (it flips) by testing permitted commands LIVE — don't trust stale "degraded" notes or a report's stale numbers (L20). Confirm by EXERCISING observed behavior, not status alone (L39). Never-exercised subsystems fail in CHAINS (s83-s91 construction: missing migration → no activation/executor → poisoned 0-task pipeline → over-strict market filter): plan for N>1 blockers and keep each probe cheap. Three ways "merged" lies: (1) a merged DAEMON fix takes effect only after the daemon RESTARTS (L39); (2) a client-side CLI panic can persist from a STALE BINARY after its fix merges — re-verify by exercising, reopen merged→new on recurrence (L42; `ship sell` confirmed crash-safe s28); (3) a merged feature with GREEN tests can still break production on SCHEMA — test DBs auto-migrate from GORM tags, production relies on hand-written migrations, so on a schema-touching `feat` re-check the earner next session and confirm the fix reached the LIVE DB (a 42703 self-heals once the column exists — leave the coordinator running, idempotent failed Saves) (L52). And a merged code fix does NOT repair the STATE the buggy binary already left behind: expect poisoned persisted rows as a SECOND, independent blocker (s87: an EXECUTING CONSTRUCTION pipeline whose tasks restart-recovery had reaped), and idempotent "resume" verbs turn that state into a permanent trap (StartOrResume returns any PLANNING/EXECUTING pipeline as-is — --depth ignored, no re-plan, nothing terminalizes a 0-task pipeline). So verify the DATA precondition (do the tasks/rows exist?) in the same breath as the code path, and put "tasks exist" BEFORE "output > 0" in the expectation chain (L60). Corollary: when a start verb fails on a market/data precondition, read the exact filter threshold in CODE before waiting for the world to change — the tolerant variant may already exist one function away (FindExportMarketBySupplyPriority vs FindExportMarketWithGoodSupply) (L62).

bd ready --type does NOT accept a comma-list: 'bd ready --type bug,feature' matches ZERO types and falsely reports 'no ready work / all blocked' (bd sp-<filed>). The shipwright prime's canonical query is BROKEN this way. ALWAYS check the queue with 'bd ready -l shipwright' (no --type) from the REPO ROOT, or run --type bug and --type feature separately. Never trust an empty result from the comma-list form.

Slow bead delivery is CPU CONTENTION + workflow overhead, NOT slow tests ('go test ./...' ~30s cold, 79 pkgs, no hangs/sleeps). ROOT CAUSE: many concurrent subagents each running the FULL suite starve the CPU -> 50-min tasks. FIXES: (1) cap concurrency at 2-3 subagents so they don't contend; (2) brief agents to test the CHANGED PACKAGE during TDD ('go test ./<pkg>/...' ~2s), full suite ONCE before gate; (3) skip test-quality-auditor for tiny/urgent fixes; (4) the gate's full-suite run is cached, fast on an unchanged tree. Do NOT 'gate it yourself' to go faster — the Admiral's directive (2026-07-09) is DELEGATE and keep the orchestrator context LEAN; self-reading diffs/build output bloats it. Orchestrator only dispatches, deploys (daemon restart), closes beads, notifies. Fable test-suite audit pending for deeper speedups + mutation-testing verdict.

captain-gate merges are now CONTAMINATION-CLEAN as of sp-ezar (2026-07-08, main ce5f46b+): SquashMerge uses commit-tree(branch^{tree}, -p HEAD) + merge --ff-only, bypassing the shared index AND the beads pre-commit hook, with a guard that ABORTS on any foreign staged file (exempting .beads/issues.jsonl root+nested). LIVE-VERIFIED: a real gated merge produced a 0-contamination commit. So shipwright can use 'captain-gate -provision -merge' normally again — the manual-FF workaround is retired. ONE EXCEPTION: sp-a3r9 (open P2) — captain-gate -provision REVERTS a worktree's regenerated proto (daemon.pb.go) to main's stale version, breaking the build for any PROTO-CHANGING bead. Until a3r9 is fixed, land proto beads by MANUAL FF: rebase onto main, 'git checkout HEAD -- <proto files>' if provision ran, verify 'go build ./... && go test ./...' by hand, then 'git merge --ff-only <branch>' (ezar's squashMergeClean merges the committed tree, so a manual FF is clean and correct). Non-proto beads: just use the gate.

Admiral hull doctrine (2026-07-09): frigate-class purchases disfavored — small cargo (60) + small fuel holds mean refuel stops eat the speed advantage, especially BURN-mode and cross-gate legs. Purchases favor heavy frames (speed + hold + tank together, e.g. HEAVY_FREIGHTER speed-36/225-cargo). Right-size operations with owned hulls before any buy.

Admiral standing order (2026-07-09): EVERY bead filed for the shipwright gets an immediate mail nudge ('gc mail send shipwright -s ... -m ... --from captain --notify') pointing at the bead id — filing alone is not delivery; the queue is only polled on their wake, a nudge wakes them. Include priority, whether it's approval-gated (human label), and where it ranks vs their current queue.

Recurring failure (2026-07-09): long-running/over-scoped shipwright subagents GRIND or STALL at the gate step — they complete the WORK (all files + tests) but never merge (hang/die at gating; sometimes --merge was omitted). trade-safety (1h+) and factory-guard (1h+) both did this. FIX: (1) scope ONE fix per agent, not bundles — bundling + mid-build interruptions caused the over-run; (2) ALWAYS put --provision --merge in the gate command (validate-only never merges: captain-gate fixer.go:60 'if !merge return'); (3) don't let an agent run >~20min without a gate — check worktree mtime; (4) RESCUE a stall by STOPPING the agent and dispatching a FRESH one to adopt its worktree (git -C <wt> diff survives the stop), rebase onto current main, and gate — a fresh agent lands the mostly-done work in MINUTES where the incumbent ground for an hour (bp6f-land, factory-guard2).

Socket & backends doctrine (consolidates L19/L30). TWO backends: the socket (health/ship/container/workflow) is the DAEMON; market/ledger/player are POSTGRES. SQLSTATE errors on the Postgres side while the socket still answers = a DB outage, NOT daemon death — keep operating on socket data. A socket hang (context deadline exceeded) can be spontaneous and may not self-recover in-session. Scope it with ONE socket probe + ONE ledger probe (if the ledger answers, the daemon isn't dead); don't hand-probe repeatedly (token burn) — record, defer, it recovers between sessions. A multi-session blackout can be an out-of-band ops event, not a code bug.

The supervisor daemon is the WATCHKEEPER (bin/watchkeeper, cmd/watchkeeper, package watchkeeper in internal/captain dir, 'watchkeeper:' log prefixes, banner 'Watchkeeper starting') since sp-t0f6 (2026-07-07). 'captain' = ONLY the agent. Deploy: make build-watchkeeper (build-captain aliases to it); launchd label REMAINS com.spacetraders.captain; log file remains captain-supervisor.log. The plist (~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.spacetraders.captain.plist) runs bin/watchkeeper and MUST carry BD_REAL=/Users/andres.dandrea/IdeaProjects/bin/bd: the bd-router shim in ~/.local/bin shadows the real bd and the plist PATH lacks ~/IdeaProjects/bin, so without BD_REAL every captain wake fails silently (gc mail -> bd list -> exit 127). Machine-local: re-add BD_REAL after any plist regen/reinstall; the same hazard hits any launchd service that calls gc. Supersedes older memories' bin/captain references.

Captain wake model (sp-sk68, live 2026-07-07): the captain OWNS its cadence — declare next-wake time / credit thresholds / interrupt-types at the end of each wake via 'spacetraders captain wake set ...' (one call, full-replace; 'captain wake show' inspects). The supervisor defers routine-success events (they queue and ride the next wake) and wakes only on anomalies or captain-set triggers. POINTER — the captain prompt template teaches the full model at prime (Wake ritual step 6); this memory just prevents re-filing.

spacetraders CLI ops (current, 2026-07-07; supersedes the old gobot-cwd rule): the CLI is on PATH with any-cwd config resolution (sp-b07s/ccd84a5) - run 'spacetraders' from anywhere; the old 'must run from gobot/' trap (SASL/connection-refused outside gobot/) is DEAD. Default player persists via 'config set-player --player-id N' (~/.spacetraders/config.json) but some verbs still demand explicit --player-id (ledger list, contract list). Treasury: 'player info' works (sp-900v) but echoes the raw player token in output until sp-byza lands - do NOT paste its output; use the ledger list balance column as the safe treasury read. Shipyard listings show prices only when one of YOUR ships is docked at that yard (an API visibility rule, not an empty yard).

Cache-desync doctrine (recurrence-watch; consolidates L32/L37/L47/L50 + L61 refresh-pool clause). `ship info` cargo/position/role can be a PHANTOM (stale cache) diverging from the SERVER, which is ground truth (delivery/sell API). It RECURS after each contract and after any purchase. The daemon now AUTO-HEALS it — sp-os5s (deployed) auto-refreshes on 4204 / cargo-mismatch / foreign-cargo / post-purchase signatures — so treat this as a recurrence-watch, not a manual routine. Symptoms: (a) a deterministic server cargo=0 failure — don't retry; (b) corrupted POSITION → API 4204 "already at destination"; (c) a phantom FOREIGN-CARGO hold silently BENCHES a hauler from the coordinator's eligible pool (looks half-full → not claimable); (d) a freshly-purchased ship caches EMPTY Role → invisible to role-based coordinators; (e) a hauler idle across sessions with stale partial cargo is a refresh candidate, not a lost ship (s81). Manual fallback if auto-heal regresses: `ship refresh --ship <sym>` (force GET /my/ships, allowlisted s32) is the in-band fix — do NOT restart a healthy daemon to clear a phantom. Desyncs strike FLEET-WIDE, so on any negotiate/workflow error naming ship state, refresh the WHOLE POOL (the daemon's dock-retry trusts the stale cache and can never self-heal on its own). If `ship refresh` is unavailable, recover POSITION by navigating to a THIRD waypoint (not the stale one, not the phantom "already-at" one). Role-visibility caveat: a phantom-cleared EXISTING-role idle hauler rejoins the eligible pool IN-LOOP with NO container restart (the contract coordinator re-discovers idle light haulers every contract-accept; s82/d-87 ran 3 contracts post-refresh), but a NEW/empty-role ship is picked up only on the coordinator's next CONTAINER RESTART. Diagnostic: a healthy daemon + RUNNING coordinator + FROZEN ledger emitting zero events was a silent error-loop — now also surfaced as a crash-LOOP detector signal (sp-no9i); check container logs. A merge self-deploy is itself a daemon event: the restart self-heals via ReleaseAllActive (open-era-scoped, sp-s7b7) and accepted contracts resume on next claim, but it reshuffles which hauler serves which stream (~5-10min) — after a deploy, verify every mid-workflow ship still has an executor.

Wake-ritual addition (2026-07-09, after a 4h contract collapse went undetected): EVERY wake, check per-ENGINE throughput from the ledger (contract_revenue rate, trading_revenue rate separately), NOT just aggregate treasury/income. Aggregate climbs can mask a dead engine — factories masked a dead contract line for 4h. Treasury-up is not health; per-line rates are. Quick check: psql sum(amount) by category over last 30-60min.

L40 [d-20,d-21,d-59] — A single 4203 fuel-crash (and transient API-error/404 bursts) is SELF-HEALING: the container auto-restarts, refuels, resumes; the coordinator re-spawns a fresh worker after a burst window. Don't intervene on the first crash (real crash-LOOPS surface as container.crashloop events, sp-no9i — see crashloop-resumes-on-deploy); escalate a ship idle >60min WITH cargo. Judge a route by NET ledger P&L, not fuel-line alarm.

register --new token print is deliberate last-resort recovery, NOT a routine echo — do NOT mask it. player_register_new.go:97 prints the agent token ONLY in the persist-FAILURE branch; the success path never prints it (token goes to DB). SpaceTraders mints an agent token once with no re-fetch, so on persist failure that terminal line is the sole surviving copy — masking it (as sp-c04g/byza-mirror tried) causes permanent identity loss. Closed sp-c04g won't-fix 2026-07-07. Token-masking (byza sp-byza) applies to ROUTINE echoes like 'player info' that run every wake, not to emergency recovery prints.

Rollover discipline defers OPEN-ENDED work to a fresh context, never a fully-specified small fix - staleness risk is misdiagnosis, not execution; a diagnosed one-liner blocking a decision chain gets fixed before handing off. And deferral-to-next-spawn is only cheap when respawn is automatic - under human-relaunch recovery, 'next session' is an unbounded delay and must be priced as such.

Orchestration lessons (2026-07-09, learned the hard way). (1) LIVENESS SIGNALS FLAP — the watchkeeper's 'STANDING SESSION DOWN <role>' log and bd's 'session died' notes are FALSE-POSITIVES (observed: captain logged DOWN while its process pid 93157 was alive; sp-s8qq + sp-snmb flipped to 'session died' x2 while their agents were alive AND completing). To check if an agent/session is alive use: process (ps for the claude/fable proc), file-mtime on its worktree (find -mmin -8), or git branch commit recency — NEVER the 'died/down' signals. Filed on sp-0d1g. (2) FRESH SPAWN vs RESUME — fresh Agent-tool subagents run autonomously to completion; a teammate RESUMED via SendMessage after it went idle/done tends to idle again after claiming instead of running the bead through (fullhold-fix did this). For a NEW bead, spawn FRESH (Agent tool) rather than SendMessage-reusing an idle agent, unless the warm context is essential. (3) OVERLAP OWNERSHIP — when two agents build the same primitive, decide ownership ONCE (by who already has the work + best design + reliability) and STICK; flip-flopping (I reversed 3x chasing a liveness ghost) wastes agent effort. The pre-dispatch mtime check is what actually prevents the merge-collision. (4) GENUINE deaths DO happen — API 'Response stalled mid-stream' yields an explicit idle_notification failureReason; those are real: relaunch fresh + salvage the dead agent's uncommitted worktree diff (git -C <wt> diff).

Long-flying CLI workflows (anything that navigates ships in-process) must run in BACKGROUND, never under a foreground shell timeout - killing the CLI mid-flight orphans any daemon-side state it persisted (claims, assignments) with no cleanup path. Before running any workflow verb, know whether it executes in-process or dispatches to a daemon container; in-process executors die with your shell.
L27 [d-7,d-8] — container-RUNNING ≠ data-populated; gate a dependent workflow on the feeder's `workflow.finished`/COMPLETED, not merely RUNNING.
L36 [d-16] — A multi-session HOLD needs a falsifiable off-ramp: use report status (L35) + an explicit exit condition. A bounded HOLD beats an open-ended one.

Spawned fix-agents share this session's $CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/tmp — leftover scratch files (e.g. a prior task's commitmsg.txt) can be read by a later agent and cause wrong commit messages/inputs. fix-r3cl hit this (read a stale hs2j commitmsg), caught+amended it. When briefing agents that write to job tmp: have them use a unique per-bead filename or pass content inline, and verify the final commit subject before merge. Not an engine bug; a harness-hygiene note.
